idx_events_created_at ON events(created_at);\n148\t    CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_events_actor ON events(actor) WHERE actor != '';\n149\t\n150\t    -- Config (Runtime)\n151\t    -- NOTE: Avoid -- `blocked_by` stores a JSON array of blocking issue IDs.\n187\t    CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS blocked_issues_cache (\n188\t        issue_id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,\n189\t        blocked_by TEXT NOT NULL,\n190\t        blocked_at DATETIME NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,\n191\t        FOREIGN KEY (issue_id) REFERENCES issues(id) ON DELETE CASCADE\n192\t    );\n193\t    CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_blocked_cache_blocked_at ON blocked_issues_cache(blocked_at);\n194\t\n195\t    -- Child Counters (for hierarchical IDs like bd-abc.1, bd-abc.2)\n196\t    CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS child_counters (\n197\t        parent_id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,\n198\t        last_child INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,\n199\t        FOREIGN KEY (parent_id) REFERENCES issues(id) ON DELETE CASCADE\n200\t    );\n201\t\n202\t    -- Critical Path Cache (for float-based ordering in claim operations)\n203\t    -- Stores the critical path float value for each bead.\n204\t    -- Lower float = more critical updated_at DATETIME NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,\n209\t        FOREIGN KEY (bead_id) REFERENCES issues(id) ON DELETE CASCADE\n210\t    );\n211\t    CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_critical_path_float ON critical_path_cache(float);\n212\t\n213\t    -- Recovery Sessions (for anomaly/recovery audit trail)\n214\t    CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS recovery_sessions (\n215\t trigger_reason TEXT,\n221\t        workspace_path TEXT,\n222\t        summary TEXT\n223\t    );\n224\t    CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_recovery_sessions_started ON ON recovery_sessions(session_type);\n227\t\n228\t    -- Anomaly Audit Records (for anomaly/recovery event tracking)\n229\t    CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS anomaly_audit (\n230\t        id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,\n231\t        correlation_id TEXT NOT NULL,\n232\t        timestamp TEXT NOT NULL,\n233\t        event_type TEXT NOT NULL,\n234\t        anomaly_type TEXT,\n235\t        recovery_strategy TEXT,\n236\t        outcome TEXT,\n237\t        evidence TEXT,\n238\t        metadata TEXT,\n239\t        created_at DATETIME NOT NULL DEFAULT (CURRENT_TIMESTAMP),\n240\t        FOREIGN KEY (correlation_id) REFERENCES recovery_sessions(correlation_id) ON DELETE CASCADE\n241\t    );\n242\t    CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_anomaly_audit_correlation ON anomaly_audit(correlation_id);\n243\t    CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_anomaly_audit_timestamp ON anomaly_audit(timestamp);\n244\t    CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_anomaly_audit_event_type ON anomaly_audit(event_type);\n245\t    CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_anomaly_audit_anomaly_type ON anomaly_audit(anomaly_type);\n246\t    CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_anomaly_audit_outcome ON anomaly_audit(outcome);\n247\t\";\n248\t\n249\t/// Split a SQL script into individual statements, respecting string literals,\n250\t/// quoted identifiers, and comments.\n251\t///\n252\t/// A naive `split(';')` breaks when SQL string literals contain semicolons\n253\t/// (e.g., `INSERT INTO t(v) VALUES('a;b')`). This function uses a small state\n254\t/// machine to track whether the current position is inside:\n255\t/// - A single-quoted string literal (`'...'`, with `''` as escape)\n256\t/// - A double-quoted identifier (`\"...\"`, with `\"\"` as escape)\n257\t/// - A line comment (`-- ...`)\n258\t/// - A block comment (`/* ... */`)\n259\t///\n260\t/// Only semicolons at the top level (outside all of the above) are treated as\n261\t/// statement terminators.\n262\tfn split_sql_statements(sql: &str) -> Vec<&str> {\n263\t    let bytes = sql.as_bytes();\n264\t    let len = bytes.len();\n265\t    let mut stmts = Vec::new();\n266\t    let mut start = 0; // byte offset where the current statement begins\n267\t    let mut i = 0;\n268\t\n269\t    // State flags — at most one is true at a time.\n270\t    let mut in_single_quote = false;\n271\t    let mut in_double_quote = false;\n272\t    let mut in_line_comment = false;\n273\t    let mut in_block_comment = false;\n274\t\n275\t    while i < len {\n276\t        let b = bytes[i];\n277\t\n278\t        // --- Line comment state ---\n279\t        if in_line_comment {\n280\t            if b == b'\\n' {\n281\t                in_line_comment = false;\n282\t            }\n283\t            i += 1;\n284\t            continue;\n285\t        }\n286\t\n287\t        // --- Block comment state ---\n288\t        if in_block_comment {\n289\t            if b == b'*' && i + 1 < len && bytes[i + 1] == b'/' {\n290\t                in_block_comment = false;\n291\t                i += 2;\n292\t            } else {\n293\t                i += 1;\n294\t            }\n295\t            continue;\n296\t        }\n297\t\n298\t        // --- Single-quoted string state ---\n299\t        if in_single_quote {\n300\t            if b == b'\\'' {\n301\t                // '' is an escaped quote inside a string literal\n302\t                if i + 1 < len && bytes[i + 1] == b'\\'' {\n303\t                    i += 2;\n304\t                } else {\n305\t                    in_single_quote = false;\n306\t                    i += 1;\n307\t                }\n308\t            } else {\n309\t                i += 1;\n310\t            }\n311\t            continue;\n312\t        }\n313\t\n314\t        // --- Double-quoted identifier state ---\n315\t        if in_double_quote {\n316\t            if b == b'\"' {\n317\t                if i + 1 < len && bytes[i + 1] == b'\"' {\n318\t                    i += 2;\n319\t                } else {\n320\t                    in_double_quote = false;\n321\t                    i += 1;\n322\t                }\n323\t            } else {\n324\t                i += 1;\n325\t            }\n326\t            continue;\n327\t        }\n328\t\n329\t        // --- Top-level parsing ---\n330\t        if b == b'\\'' {\n331\t            in_single_quote = true;\n332\t            i += 1;\n333\t        } else if b == b'\"' {\n334\t            in_double_quote = true;\n335\t            i += 1;\n336\t        } else if b == b'-' && i + 1 < len && bytes[i + 1] == b'-' {\n337\t            in_line_comment = true;\n338\t            i += 2;\n339\t        } else if b == b'/' && i + 1 < len && bytes[i + 1] == b'*' {\n340\t            in_block_comment = true;\n341\t            i += 2;\n342\t        } else if b == b';' {\n343\t            // Statement terminator at top level.\n344\t            let stmt = &sql[start..i];\n345\t            if !stmt.trim().is_empty() {\n346\t                stmts.push(stmt.trim());\n347\t            }\n348\t            start = i + 1;\n349\t            i += 1;\n350\t        } else {\n351\t            i += 1;\n352\t        }\n353\t    }\n354\t\n355\t    // Trailing statement without a final semicolon.\n356\t    if start < len {\n357\t        let stmt = &sql[start..len];\n358\t        if !stmt.trim().is_empty() {\n359\t            stmts.push(stmt.trim());\n360\t        }\n361\t    }\n362\t\n363\t    stmts\n364\t}\n365\t\n366\t/// Execute multiple SQL statements separated by semicolons.\n367\tfn execute_batch(conn: &rusqlite::Connection, sql: &str) -> anyhow::Result<()> {\n368\t    conn.execute_batch(sql)?;\n369\t    Ok(())\n370\t}\n371\t\n372\tpub fn apply_schema(conn: &rusqlite::Connection) -> anyhow::Result<()> {\n373\t    execute_batch(conn, SCHEMA_SQL)?;\n374\t    Ok(())\n375\t}\n376\t\n377\tpub fn ensure_wal_mode(conn: &rusqlite::Connection) -> anyhow::Result<()> {\n378\t    // Use execute_batch so PRAGMAs that return rows don't cause errors\n379\t    conn.execute_batch(\n380\t        \"PRAGMA journal_mode = WAL;\n381\t         PRAGMA foreign_keys = ON;\n382\t         PRAGMA busy_timeout = 30000;\n383\t         PRAGMA cache_size = -8000;\n384\t         PRAGMA synchronous = NORMAL;\"\n385\t    )?;\n386\t    Ok(())\n387\t}\n388\t"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"66df31d4-ec3e-4dfe-955b-de4eea8c5ecd","uuid":"f21f187b-821e-4fd3-bbeb-9b0b27fd5ccf","timestamp":"2026-04-30T17:16:00.821Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/home/coding/bead-forge/src/storage/schema.rs","content":"/// The complete SQL schema for the beads database.\n/// Schema matches classic bd (Go) for interoperability.\npub const SCHEMA_SQL: &str = r\"\n    -- Issues table\n    -- Note: TEXT fields use DEFAULT '' for bd (Go) compatibility.\n    -- bd's sql.Scan doesn't handle NULL well when scanning into string fields.\n    -- Closed-at invariant is enforced by the CHECK clause below.\n    CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS issues (\n        id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,\n        content_hash TEXT,\n        title TEXT NOT NULL CHECK(length(title) <= 500),\n        description TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '',\n        design TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '',\n        acceptance_criteria TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '',\n        notes TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '',\n        status TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'open',\n        priority INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 2 CHECK(priority >= 0 AND priority <= 4),\n        issue_type TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'task',\n        assignee TEXT,\n        owner TEXT DEFAULT '',\n        estimated_minutes INTEGER,\n        created_at DATETIME NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,\n        created_by TEXT DEFAULT '',\n        updated_at DATETIME NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,\n        closed_at DATETIME,\n        close_reason TEXT DEFAULT '',\n        closed_by_session TEXT DEFAULT '',\n        due_at DATETIME,\n        defer_until DATETIME,\n        external_ref TEXT,\n        source_system TEXT DEFAULT '',\n        source_repo TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '.',\n        deleted_at DATETIME,\n        deleted_by TEXT DEFAULT '',\n        delete_reason TEXT DEFAULT '',\n        original_type TEXT DEFAULT '',\n        compaction_level INTEGER DEFAULT 0,\n        compacted_at DATETIME,\n        compacted_at_commit TEXT,\n        original_size INTEGER,\n        sender TEXT DEFAULT '',\n        ephemeral INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,\n        pinned INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,\n        is_template INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,\n        CHECK (\n            (status = 'closed' AND closed_at IS NOT NULL) OR\n            (status = 'tombstone') OR\n            (status NOT IN ('closed', 'tombstone') AND closed_at IS NULL)\n        )\n    );\n\n    -- Primary access patterns\n    CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_issues_status ON issues(status);\n    CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_issues_priority ON issues(priority);\n    CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_issues_issue_type ON issues(issue_type);\n    CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_issues_assignee ON issues(assignee) WHERE assignee IS NOT NULL;\n    CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_issues_created_at ON issues(created_at);\n    CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_issues_updated_at ON issues(updated_at);\n\n    -- Export/sync patterns\n    CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_issues_content_hash ON issues(content_hash);\n    CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_issues_external_ref ON issues(external_ref) WHERE external_ref IS NOT NULL;\n    CREATE UNIQUE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_issues_external_ref_unique ON issues(external_ref) WHERE external_ref IS NOT NULL;\n\n    -- Special states\n    CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_issues_ephemeral ON issues(ephemeral) WHERE ephemeral = 1;\n    CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_issues_pinned ON issues(pinned) WHERE pinned = 1;\n    CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_issues_tombstone ON issues(status) WHERE status = 'tombstone';\n\n    -- Time-based\n    CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_issues_due_at ON issues(due_at) WHERE due_at IS NOT NULL;\n    CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_issues_defer_until ON issues(defer_until) WHERE defer_until IS NOT NULL;\n\n    -- Ready work composite index (most important for performance)\n    CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_issues_ready\n        ON issues(status, priority, created_at)\n        WHERE status = 'open'\n        AND ephemeral = 0\n        AND pinned = 0\n        AND is_template = 0;\n\n    -- Common active list path: non-terminal issues sorted by priority/created_at\n    CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_issues_list_active_order\n        ON issues(priority, created_at DESC)\n        WHERE status NOT IN ('closed', 'tombstone')\n        AND (is_template = 0 OR is_template IS NULL);\n\n    -- Dependencies\n    CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS dependencies (\n        issue_id TEXT NOT NULL,\n        depends_on_id TEXT NOT NULL,\n        type TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'blocks',\n        created_at DATETIME NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,\n        created_by TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '',\n        metadata TEXT DEFAULT '{}',\n        thread_id TEXT DEFAULT '',\n        PRIMARY KEY (issue_id, depends_on_id),\n        FOREIGN KEY (issue_id) REFERENCES issues(id) ON DELETE CASCADE\n        -- Note: depends_on_id FK intentionally removed to allow external issue references\n    );\n    CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_dependencies_issue ON dependencies(issue_id);\n    CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_dependencies_depends_on ON dependencies(depends_on_id);\n    CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_dependencies_type ON dependencies(type);\n    CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_dependencies_depends_on_type ON dependencies(depends_on_id, type);\n    CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_dependencies_thread ON dependencies(thread_id) WHERE thread_id != '';\n    -- Composite for blocking lookups\n    CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_dependencies_blocking\n        ON dependencies(depends_on_id, issue_id)\n        WHERE (type = 'blocks' OR type = 'parent-child' OR type = 'conditional-blocks' OR type = 'waits-for');\n\n    -- Labels\n    CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS labels (\n        issue_id TEXT NOT NULL,\n        label TEXT NOT NULL,\n        PRIMARY KEY (issue_id, label),\n        FOREIGN KEY (issue_id) REFERENCES issues(id) ON DELETE CASCADE\n    );\n    CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_labels_label ON labels(label);\n    CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_labels_issue ON labels(issue_id);\n\n    -- Comments\n    CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS comments (\n        id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,\n        issue_id TEXT NOT NULL,\n        author TEXT NOT NULL,\n        text TEXT NOT NULL,\n        created_at DATETIME NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,\n        FOREIGN KEY (issue_id) REFERENCES issues(id) ON DELETE CASCADE\n    );\n    CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_comments_issue ON comments(issue_id);\n    CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_comments_created_at ON comments(created_at);\n\n    -- Events (Audit)\n    CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS events (\n        id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,\n        issue_id TEXT NOT NULL,\n        event_type TEXT NOT NULL,\n        actor TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '',\n        old_value TEXT,\n        new_value TEXT,\n        comment TEXT,\n        created_at DATETIME NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,\n        FOREIGN KEY (issue_id) REFERENCES issues(id) ON DELETE CASCADE\n    );\n    CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_events_issue ON events(issue_id);\n    CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_events_type ON events(event_type);\n    CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_events_created_at ON events(created_at);\n    CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_events_actor ON events(actor) WHERE actor != '';\n\n    -- Config (Runtime)\n    -- NOTE: Avoid PRIMARY KEY/UNIQUE constraints here because the current\n    -- storage engine does not reliably maintain unique autoindexes.\n    -- Application code enforces key replacement via DELETE + INSERT.\n    CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS config (\n        key TEXT NOT NULL,\n        value TEXT NOT NULL\n    );\n    CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_config_key ON config(key);\n\n    -- Metadata\n    -- Same rationale as config: keep it as key-value with explicit index.\n    CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS metadata (\n        key TEXT NOT NULL,\n        value TEXT NOT NULL\n    );\n    CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_metadata_key ON metadata(key);\n\n    -- Dirty Issues (for export)\n    CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS dirty_issues (\n        issue_id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,\n        marked_at DATETIME NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,\n        FOREIGN KEY (issue_id) REFERENCES issues(id) ON DELETE CASCADE\n    );\n    CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_dirty_issues_marked_at ON dirty_issues(marked_at);\n\n    -- Export Hashes (for incremental export)\n    CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS export_hashes (\n        issue_id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,\n        content_hash TEXT NOT NULL,\n        exported_at DATETIME NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,\n        FOREIGN KEY (issue_id) REFERENCES issues(id) ON DELETE CASCADE\n    );\n\n    -- Blocked Issues Cache (Materialized view)\n    -- Rebuilt on dependency or status changes.\n    -- `blocked_by` stores a JSON array of blocking issue IDs.\n    CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS blocked_issues_cache (\n        issue_id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,\n        blocked_by TEXT NOT NULL,\n        blocked_at DATETIME NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,\n        FOREIGN KEY (issue_id) REFERENCES issues(id) ON DELETE CASCADE\n    );\n    CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_blocked_cache_blocked_at ON blocked_issues_cache(blocked_at);\n\n    -- Child Counters (for hierarchical IDs like bd-abc.1, bd-abc.2)\n    CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS child_counters (\n        parent_id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,\n        last_child INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,\n        FOREIGN KEY (parent_id) REFERENCES issues(id) ON DELETE CASCADE\n    );\n\n    -- Critical Path Cache (for float-based ordering in claim operations)\n    -- Stores the critical path float value for each bead.\n    -- Lower float = more critical (closer to root of critical path).\n    CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS critical_path_cache (\n        bead_id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,\n        float REAL NOT NULL DEFAULT 999999,\n        updated_at DATETIME NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,\n        FOREIGN KEY (bead_id) REFERENCES issues(id) ON DELETE CASCADE\n    );\n    CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_critical_path_float ON critical_path_cache(float);\n\n    -- Recovery Sessions (for anomaly/recovery audit trail)\n    CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS recovery_sessions (\n        correlation_id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,\n        started_at TEXT NOT NULL,\n        completed_at TEXT,\n        final_outcome TEXT,\n        session_type TEXT NOT NULL,\n        trigger_reason TEXT,\n        workspace_path TEXT,\n        summary TEXT\n    );\n    CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_recovery_sessions_started ON recovery_sessions(started_at);\n    CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_recovery_sessions_outcome ON recovery_sessions(final_outcome);\n    CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_recovery_sessions_type ON recovery_sessions(session_type);\n\n    -- Anomaly Audit Records (for anomaly/recovery event tracking)\n    CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS anomaly_audit (\n        id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,\n        correlation_id TEXT NOT NULL,\n        timestamp TEXT NOT NULL,\n        event_type TEXT NOT NULL,\n        anomaly_type TEXT,\n        recovery_strategy TEXT,\n        outcome TEXT,\n        evidence TEXT,\n        metadata TEXT,\n        created_at DATETIME NOT NULL DEFAULT (CURRENT_TIMESTAMP),\n        FOREIGN KEY (correlation_id) REFERENCES recovery_sessions(correlation_id) ON DELETE CASCADE\n    );\n    CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_anomaly_audit_correlation ON anomaly_audit(correlation_id);\n    CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_anomaly_audit_timestamp ON anomaly_audit(timestamp);\n    CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_anomaly_audit_event_type ON anomaly_audit(event_type);\n    CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_anomaly_audit_anomaly_type ON anomaly_audit(anomaly_type);\n    CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_anomaly_audit_outcome ON anomaly_audit(outcome);\n\";\n\n/// Split a SQL script into individual statements, respecting string literals,\n/// quoted identifiers, and comments.\n///\n/// A naive `split(';')` breaks when SQL string literals contain semicolons\n/// (e.g., `INSERT INTO t(v) VALUES('a;b')`). This function uses a small state\n/// machine to track whether the current position is inside:\n/// - A single-quoted string literal (`'...'`, with `''` as escape)\n/// - A double-quoted identifier (`\"...\"`, with `\"\"` as escape)\n/// - A line comment (`-- ...`)\n/// - A block comment (`/* ... */`)\n///\n/// Only semicolons at the top level (outside all of the above) are treated as\n/// statement terminators.\nfn split_sql_statements(sql: &str) -> Vec<&str> {\n    let bytes = sql.as_bytes();\n    let len = bytes.len();\n    let mut stmts = Vec::new();\n    let mut start = 0; // byte offset where the current statement begins\n    let mut i = 0;\n\n    // State flags — at most one is true at a time.\n    let mut in_single_quote = false;\n    let mut in_double_quote = false;\n    let mut in_line_comment = false;\n    let mut in_block_comment = false;\n\n    while i < len {\n        let b = bytes[i];\n\n        // --- Line comment state ---\n        if in_line_comment {\n            if b == b'\\n' {\n                in_line_comment = false;\n            }\n            i += 1;\n            continue;\n        }\n\n        // --- Block comment state ---\n        if in_block_comment {\n            if b == b'*' && i + 1 < len && bytes[i + 1] == b'/' {\n                in_block_comment = false;\n                i += 2;\n            } else {\n                i += 1;\n            }\n            continue;\n        }\n\n        // --- Single-quoted string state ---\n        if in_single_quote {\n            if b == b'\\'' {\n                // '' is an escaped quote inside a string literal\n                if i + 1 < len && bytes[i + 1] == b'\\'' {\n                    i += 2;\n                } else {\n                    in_single_quote = false;\n                    i += 1;\n                }\n            } else {\n                i += 1;\n            }\n            continue;\n        }\n\n        // --- Double-quoted identifier state ---\n        if in_double_quote {\n            if b == b'\"' {\n                if i + 1 < len && bytes[i + 1] == b'\"' {\n                    i += 2;\n                } else {\n                    in_double_quote = false;\n                    i += 1;\n                }\n            } else {\n                i += 1;\n            }\n            continue;\n        }\n\n        // --- Top-level parsing ---\n        if b == b'\\'' {\n            in_single_quote = true;\n            i += 1;\n        } else if b == b'\"' {\n            in_double_quote = true;\n            i += 1;\n        } else if b == b'-' && i + 1 < len && bytes[i + 1] == b'-' {\n            in_line_comment = true;\n            i += 2;\n        } else if b == b'/' && i + 1 < len && bytes[i + 1] == b'*' {\n            in_block_comment = true;\n            i += 2;\n        } else if b == b';' {\n            // Statement terminator at top level.\n            let stmt = &sql[start..i];\n            if !stmt.trim().is_empty() {\n                stmts.push(stmt.trim());\n            }\n            start = i + 1;\n            i += 1;\n        } else {\n            i += 1;\n        }\n    }\n\n    // Trailing statement without a final semicolon.\n    if start < len {\n        let stmt = &sql[start..len];\n        if !stmt.trim().is_empty() {\n            stmts.push(stmt.trim());\n        }\n    }\n\n    stmts\n}\n\n/// Execute multiple SQL statements separated by semicolons.\nfn execute_batch(conn: &rusqlite::Connection, sql: &str) -> anyhow::Result<()> {\n    conn.execute_batch(sql)?;\n    Ok(())\n}\n\npub fn apply_schema(conn: &rusqlite::Connection) -> anyhow::Result<()> {\n    execute_batch(conn, SCHEMA_SQL)?;\n    Ok(())\n}\n\npub fn ensure_wal_mode(conn: &rusqlite::Connection) -> anyhow::Result<()> {\n {"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_464756f2f8164404b7939386","type":"tool_result","content":"1\tuse anyhow::Result;\n2\tuse chrono::{DateTime, Duration, Utc};\n3\tuse rusqlite::{params, Connection};\n4\tuse serde::Serialize;\n5\t\n6\t/// Result of a claim operation\n7\t#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\n8\tpub struct ClaimResult {\n9\t    pub bead_id: String,\n10\t    pub reclaimed: usize,\n11\t}\n12\t\n13\t/// A bead with its score for ready/claim operations\n14\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize)]\n15\tpub struct ScoredBead {\n16\t    pub id: String,\n17\t    pub title: String,\n18\t    pub status: String,\n19\t    pub priority: i32,\n20\t    pub downstream_impact: i64,\n21\t    pub critical_float: f64,\n22\t    pub created_at: String,\n23\t}\n24\t\n25\t/// Atomically claim a bead for a worker.\n26\t///\n27\t/// This function performs the following in a single IMMEDIATE transaction:\n28\t/// 1. Reclaim stale in_progress beads (older than claim_ttl_minutes) back to open\n29\t/// 2. Select candidates with downstream_impact + critical_float scoring\n30\t/// 3. Update the winner to in_progress with assignee=worker\n31\t/// 4. Insert an event\n32\t/// 5. Mark the bead as dirty\n33\t/// 6. Commit\n34\t///\n35\t/// # Arguments\n36\t/// * `tx` - The transaction to use (must be an IMMEDIATE transaction)\n37\t/// * `worker` - The worker ID claiming the bead\n38\t/// * `claim_ttl_minutes` - TTL in minutes after which in_progress beads are reclaimed\n39\t/// * `now` - Current timestamp for staleness calculation\n40\t///\n41\t/// # Returns\n42\t/// * `Ok(Some(claim_result))` - A bead was claimed\n43\t/// * `Ok(None)` - No beads available to claim\n44\t/// * `Err(e)` - Transaction error\n45\tpub fn claim(\n46\t    tx: &Connection,\n47\t    worker: &str,\n48\t    claim_ttl_minutes: i64,\n49\t    now: DateTime<Utc>,\n50\t) -> Result<Option<ClaimResult>> {\n51\t    // Step 1: Reclaim stale in_progress beads\n52\t    let stale_cutoff = now - Duration::minutes(claim_ttl_minutes);\n53\t    let reclaimed = tx.execute(\n54\t        \"UPDATE issues\n55\t         SET status = 'open', assignee = NULL, updated_at = ?\n56\t         WHERE status = 'in_progress'\n57\t           AND updated_at < ?\",\n58\t        params![now.to_rfc3339(), stale_cutoff.to_rfc3339()],\n59\t    )?;\n60\t\n61\t    // Step 2: Find candidate beads with impact scoring\n62\t    // Score = downstream_impact + (critical_float / 1000.0)\n63\t    // downstream_impact = count of beads blocked by this one\n64\t    // critical_float = from critical_path_cache (lower is more critical)\n65\t    let mut stmt = tx.prepare(\n66\t        \"SELECT i.id,\n67\t                COALESCE(COUNT(d.issue_id), 0) as downstream_impact,\n68\t                COALESCE(c.float, 999999) as critical_float,\n69\t                i.priority\n70\t         FROM issues i\n71\t         LEFT JOIN dependencies d ON d.depends_on_id = i.id AND d.type IN ('blocks', 'parent-child', 'conditional-blocks', 'waits-for')\n72\t         LEFT JOIN critical_path_cache c ON c.bead_id = i.id\n73\t         WHERE i.status = 'open'\n74\t           AND i.ephemeral = 0\n75\t           AND i.pinned = 0\n76\t           AND i.is_template = 0\n77\t           AND i.deleted_at IS NULL\n78\t         GROUP BY i.id\n79\t         ORDER BY\n80\t             downstream_impact DESC,\n81\t             critical_float ASC,\n82\t             i.priority ASC,\n83\t             i.created_at ASC\n84\t         LIMIT 1\",\n85\t    )?;\n86\t\n87\t    let mut rows = stmt.query([])?;\n88\t\n89\t    if let Some(row) = rows.next()? {\n90\t        let bead_id: String = row.get(0)?;\n91\t\n92\t        // Step 3: Update the winner to in_progress\n93\t        tx.execute(\n94\t            \"UPDATE issues\n95\t             SET status = 'in_progress', assignee = ?, updated_at = ?\n96\t             WHERE id = ?\",\n97\t            params![worker, now.to_rfc3339(), &bead_id],\n98\t        )?;\n99\t\n100\t        // Step 4: Insert event\n101\t        tx.execute(\n102\t            \"INSERT INTO events (issue_id, event_type, actor, new_value, created_at)\n103\t             VALUES (?, 'assignee_changed', '', ?, ?)\",\n104\t            params![&bead_id, worker, now.to_rfc3339()],\n105\t        )?;\n106\t\n107\t        // Step 5: Mark as dirty\n108\t        tx.execute(\n109\t            \"INSERT OR REPLACE INTO dirty_issues (issue_id, marked_at)\n110\t             VALUES (?, ?)\",\n111\t            params![&bead_id, now.to_rfc3339()],\n112\t        )?;\n113\t\n114\t        Ok(Some(ClaimResult {\n115\t            bead_id,\n116\t            reclaimed,\n117\t        }))\n118\t    } else {\n119\t        Ok(None)\n120\t    }\n121\t}\n122\t\n123\t/// Get ready candidates using the same scoring logic as claim().\n124\t///\n125\t/// This returns a list of beads that would be considered for claiming,\n126\t/// ordered by the same scoring formula:\n127\t/// - downstream_impact DESC (more blocking = higher priority)\n128\t/// - critical_float ASC (lower float = more critical)\n129\t/// - priority ASC (0=Critical, 4=Backlog)\n130\t/// - created_at ASC (FIFO tiebreaker)\n131\t///\n132\t/// # Arguments\n133\t/// * `tx` - The transaction to use\n134\t/// * `limit` - Maximum number of candidates to return\n135\t///\n136\t/// # Returns\n137\t/// * `Ok(Vec<ScoredBead>)` - List of scored bead candidates\n138\tpub fn get_ready_candidates(tx: &Connection, limit: usize) -> Result<Vec<ScoredBead>> {\n139\t    let mut stmt = tx.prepare(\n140\t        \"SELECT i.id, i.title, i.status, i.priority,\n141\t                COALESCE(COUNT(d.issue_id), 0) as downstream_impact,\n142\t                COALESCE(c.float, 999999) as critical_float,\n143\t                i.created_at\n144\t         FROM issues i\n145\t         LEFT JOIN dependencies d ON d.depends_on_id = i.id AND d.type IN ('blocks', 'parent-child', 'conditional-blocks', 'waits-for')\n146\t         LEFT JOIN critical_path_cache c ON c.bead_id = i.id\n147\t         WHERE i.status = 'open'\n148\t           AND i.ephemeral = 0\n149\t           AND i.pinned = 0\n150\t           AND i.is_template = 0\n151\t           AND i.deleted_at IS NULL\n152\t         GROUP BY i.id\n153\t         ORDER BY\n154\t             downstream_impact DESC,\n155\t             critical_float anyhow::Result;\nuse chrono::{DateTime, Duration, Utc};\nuse rusqlite::{params, Connection};\nuse serde::Serialize;\n\n/// Result of a claim operation\n#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\npub struct ClaimResult {\n    pub bead_id: String,\n    pub reclaimed: usize,\n}\n\n/// A bead with its score for ready/claim operations\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize)]\npub struct ScoredBead {\n    pub id: String,\n    pub title: String,\n    pub status: String,\n    pub priority: i32,\n    pub downstream_impact: i64,\n    pub critical_float: f64,\n    pub created_at: String,\n}\n\n/// Atomically claim a bead for a worker.\n///\n/// This function performs the following in a single IMMEDIATE transaction:\n/// 1. Reclaim stale in_progress beads (older than claim_ttl_minutes) back to open\n/// 2. Select candidates with downstream_impact + critical_float scoring\n/// 3. Update the winner to in_progress with assignee=worker\n/// 4. Insert an event\n/// 5. Mark the bead as dirty\n/// 6. Commit\n///\n/// # Arguments\n/// * `tx` - The transaction to use (must be an IMMEDIATE transaction)\n/// * `worker` - The worker ID claiming the bead\n/// * `claim_ttl_minutes` - TTL in minutes after which in_progress beads are reclaimed\n/// * `now` - Current timestamp for staleness calculation\n///\n/// # Returns\n/// * `Ok(Some(claim_result))` - A bead was claimed\n/// * `Ok(None)` - No beads available to claim\n/// * `Err(e)` - Transaction error\npub fn claim(\n    tx: &Connection,\n    worker: &str,\n    claim_ttl_minutes: i64,\n    now: DateTime<Utc>,\n) -> Result<Option<ClaimResult>> {\n    // Step 1: Reclaim stale in_progress beads\n    let stale_cutoff = now - Duration::minutes(claim_ttl_minutes);\n    let reclaimed = tx.execute(\n        \"UPDATE issues\n         SET status = 'open', assignee = NULL, updated_at = ?\n         WHERE status = 'in_progress'\n           AND updated_at < ?\",\n        params![now.to_rfc3339(), stale_cutoff.to_rfc3339()],\n    )?;\n\n    // Step 2: Find candidate beads with impact scoring\n    // Score = downstream_impact + (critical_float / 1000.0)\n    // downstream_impact = count of beads blocked by this one\n    // critical_float = from critical_path_cache (lower is more critical)\n    let mut stmt = tx.prepare(\n        \"SELECT i.id,\n                COALESCE(COUNT(d.issue_id), 0) as downstream_impact,\n                COALESCE(c.float, 999999) as critical_float,\n                i.priority\n         FROM issues i\n         LEFT JOIN dependencies d ON d.depends_on_id = i.id AND d.type IN ('blocks', 'parent-child', 'conditional-blocks', 'waits-for')\n         LEFT JOIN critical_path_cache c ON c.bead_id = i.id\n         WHERE i.status = 'open'\n           AND i.ephemeral = 0\n           AND i.pinned = 0\n           AND i.is_template = 0\n           AND i.deleted_at IS NULL\n         GROUP BY i.id\n         ORDER BY\n             downstream_impact DESC,\n             critical_float ASC,\n             i.priority ASC,\n             i.created_at ASC\n         LIMIT 1\",\n    )?;\n\n    let mut rows = stmt.query([])?;\n\n    if let Some(row) = rows.next()? {\n        let bead_id: String = row.get(0)?;\n\n        // Step 3: Update the winner to in_progress\n        tx.execute(\n            \"UPDATE issues\n             SET status = 'in_progress', assignee = ?, updated_at = ?\n             WHERE id = ?\",\n            params![worker, now.to_rfc3339(), &bead_id],\n        )?;\n\n        // Step 4: Insert event\n        tx.execute(\n            \"INSERT INTO events (issue_id, event_type, actor, new_value, created_at)\n             VALUES (?, 'assignee_changed', '', ?, ?)\",\n            params![&bead_id, worker, now.to_rfc3339()],\n        )?;\n\n        // Step 5: Mark as dirty\n        tx.execute(\n            \"INSERT OR REPLACE INTO dirty_issues (issue_id, marked_at)\n             VALUES (?, ?)\",\n            params![&bead_id, now.to_rfc3339()],\n        )?;\n\n        Ok(Some(ClaimResult {\n            bead_id,\n            reclaimed,\n        }))\n    } else {\n        Ok(None)\n    }\n}\n\n/// Get ready candidates using the same scoring logic as claim().\n///\n/// This returns a list of beads that would be considered for claiming,\n/// ordered by the same scoring formula:\n/// - downstream_impact DESC (more blocking = higher priority)\n/// - critical_float ASC (lower float = more critical)\n/// - priority ASC (0=Critical, 4=Backlog)\n/// - created_at ASC (FIFO tiebreaker)\n///\n/// # Arguments\n/// * `tx` - The transaction to use\n/// * `limit` - Maximum number of candidates to return\n///\n/// # Returns\n/// * `Ok(Vec<ScoredBead>)` - List of scored bead candidates\npub fn get_ready_candidates(tx: &Connection, limit: usize) -> Result<Vec<ScoredBead>> {\n    let mut stmt = tx.prepare(\n        \"SELECT i.id, i.title, i.status, i.priority,\n                COALESCE(COUNT(d.issue_id), 0) as downstream_impact,\n                COALESCE(c.float, 999999) as critical_float,\n                i.created_at\n         FROM issues i\n         LEFT JOIN dependencies d ON d.depends_on_id = i.id AND d.type IN ('blocks', 'parent-child', 'conditional-blocks', 'waits-for')\n         LEFT JOIN critical_path_cache c ON c.bead_id = i.id\n         WHERE i.status = 'open'\n           AND i.ephemeral = 0\n           AND i.pinned = 0\n           AND i.is_template = 0\n           AND i.deleted_at IS NULL\n         GROUP BY i.id\n         ORDER BY\n             downstream_impact DESC,\n             critical_float ASC,\n             i.priority ASC,\n             i.created_at ASC\n         LIMIT ?1\",\n    )?;\n\n    let mut rows = stmt.query(params![limit as i64])?;\n    let mut candidates = Vec::new();\n\n    while let Some(row) = rows.next()? {\n        candidates.push(ScoredBead {\n            id: row.get(0)?,\n            title: row.get(1)?,\n            status: row.get(2)?,\n            priority: row.get(3)?,\n            downstream_impact: row.get(4)?,\n            critical_float: row.get(5)?,\n            created_at: row.get(6)?,\n        });\n    }\n\n    Ok(candidates)\n}\n\n#[cfg(test)]\nmod tests {\n    use super::*;\n    use crate::storage::Storage;\n    use crate::model::{Issue, Status};\n\n    fn setup_test_db() -> (tempfile::NamedTempFile, Storage) {\n        let temp_file = tempfile::NamedTempFile::new().unwrap();\n        let storage = Storage::open(temp_file.path()).unwrap();\n        (temp_file, storage)\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_claim_basic() {\n        let (_temp, mut storage) = setup_test_db();\n\n        // Create an open bead\n        let issue = Issue::new(\"bf-test1\".to_string(), \"Test bead\".to_string(), \".\".to_string());\n        storage.create_issue(&issue).unwrap();\n\n        // Claim it\n        let result = storage.with_immediate_transaction(|tx| {\n            claim(tx, \"worker1\", 30, Utc::now())\n        }).unwrap();\n\n        assert!(result.is_some());\n        let claim_result = result.unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(claim_result.bead_id, \"bf-test1\");\n        assert_eq!(claim_result.reclaimed, 0);\n\n        // Verify the bead is now in_progress\n        let updated = storage.get_issue(\"bf-test1\").unwrap().unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(updated.status, Status::InProgress);\n        assert_eq!(updated.assignee.as_ref().unwrap(), \"worker1\");\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_claim_no_candidates() {\n        let (_temp, mut storage) = setup_test_db();\n\n        // No beads available\n        let result = storage.with_immediate_transaction(|tx| {\n            claim(tx, \"worker1\", 30, Utc::now())\n        }).unwrap();\n\n        assert!(result.is_none());\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_claim_reclaims_stale() {\n        let (_temp, mut storage) = setup_test_db();\n\n        // Create an in_progress bead with old updated_at\n        let mut issue = Issue::new(\"bf-stale\".to_string(), \"Stale bead\".to_string(), \".\".to_string());\n        issue.status = Status::InProgress;\n        issue.assignee = Some(\"worker_old\".to_string());\n        issue.updated_at = Utc::now() - Duration::minutes(60);\n        storage.create_issue(&issue).unwrap();\n\n        // Create an open bead\n        let issue2 = Issue::new(\"bf-open\".to_string(), \"Open bead\".to_string(), \".\".to_string());\n        storage.create_issue(&issue2).unwrap();\n\n        // Claim with 30 min TTL - should reclaim the stale one\n        let result = storage.with_immediate_transaction(|tx| {\n            claim(tx, \"worker_new\", 30, Utc::now())\n        }).unwrap();\n\n        assert!(result.is_some());\n        let claim_result = result.unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(claim_result.reclaimed, 1);\n\n        // The open bead should be claimed (not the stale one, since it was reclaimed to open)\n        // After reclaim, the stale bead is open again, so it could be claimed too\n        // But the open bead has priority by created_at order\n    }\n}\n","numLines":256,"startLine":1,"totalLines":256}}}

let conn = self.conn.lock().unwrap();\n43\t        let tx = conn.unchecked_transaction()?;\n44\t        match f(&tx) {\n45\t            Ok(result) => {\n46\t                tx.commit()?;\n47\t                drop(conn);\n48\t                Ok(result)\n49\t            }\n50\t            Err(e) => {\n51\t                let _ = tx.rollback();\n52\t                Err(e)\n53\t            }\n54\t        }\n55\t    }\n56\t    pub fn with_immediate_transaction<T, F>(&self, f: F) -> Result<T>\n57\t    where\n58\t        F: Fn(&Connection) -> Result<T>,\n59\t    {\n60\t        let mut attempt = 0;\n61\t        loop {\n62\t            let outcome = {\n63\t                let conn = self.conn.lock().unwrap();\n64\t                match conn.execute_batch(\"BEGIN IMMEDIATE\") {\n65\t                    Err(e) if is_busy_error(&e) && attempt < MAX_RETRIES => None,\n66\t                    Err(e) => return Err(e.into()),\n67\t                    Ok(_) => {\n68\t                        let r = f(&conn);\n69\t                        match &r {\n70\t                            Ok(_) => { let _ = conn.execute_batch(\"COMMIT\"); }\n71\t                            Err(_) => { let _ = conn.execute_batch(\"ROLLBACK\"); }\n72\t                        }\n73\t                        Some(r)\n74\t                    }\n75\t                }\n76\t            };\n77\t            match outcome {\n78\t                Some(r) => return r,\n79\t                None => {\n80\t                    attempt += 1;\n81\t                    std::thread::sleep(Duration::from_millis(RETRY_BASE_MS * attempt as u64));\n82\t                }\n83\t            }\n84\t        }\n85\t    }\n86\t\n87\t    pub fn get_issue(&self, id: &str) -> search_issues(&self, query: Option<&str>, status: &[Status], issue_type: &[IssueType], assignee: Option<&str>, labels: &[String], priority_min: Option<i32>, priority_max: Option<i32>, limit: usize) -> Result<Vec<Issue>> {\n564\t        let mut sql = String::from(\n565\t            \"SELECT DISTINCT i.id, i.content_hash, i.title, i.description, i.design, i.acceptance_criteria, i.notes,\n566\t                    i.status, i.priority, i.issue_type, i.assignee, i.owner, i.estimated_minutes,\n567\t                    i.created_at, i.created_by, i.updated_at, i.closed_at, i.close_reason,\n568\t                    i.closed_by_session, i.due_at, i.defer_until, i.external_ref, i.source_system,\n569\t                    i.source_repo, i.deleted_at, i.deleted_by, i.delete_reason, i.original_type,\n570\t                    i.compaction_level, i.compacted_at, i.compacted_at_commit, i.original_size,\n571\t                    i.sender, i.ephemeral, i.pinned, i.is_template\n572\t             FROM issues i\n573\t             LEFT JOIN labels l ON i.id = l.issue_id\n574\t             WHERE i.deleted_at IS NULL\",\n575\t        );\n576\t        let mut params = Vec::new();\n577\t        let mut params.push(format!(\"%{}%\", q));\n581\t            params.push(format!(\"%{}%\", q));\n582\t            param_idx += 2;\n583\t        }\n584\t        for s in status {\n585\t            sql.push_str(&format!(\" AND i.status = ?{}\", param_idx));\n586\t            params.push(s.to_string());\n587\t            param_idx += 1;\n588\t        }\n589\t        for t in issue_type {\n590\t            sql.push_str(&format!(\" AND i.issue_type = ?{}\", param_idx));\n591\t            params.push(t.to_string());\n592\t            param_idx += 1;\n593\t        }\n594\t        if let Some(a) = assignee {\n595\t            sql.push_str(&format!(\" AND i.assignee = ?{}\", param_idx));\n596\t            params.push(a.to_string());\n597\t            param_idx += 1;\n598\t        }\n599\t        if !labels.is_empty() {\n600\t            let label_conditions: Vec<String> = labels.iter().enumerate().map(|(i, _)| format!(\"l.label = ?{}\", param_idx + i)).collect();\n601\t            sql.push_str(&format!(\" AND ({}) \", label_conditions.join(\" OR \")));\n602\t            for label in labels {\n603\t                params.push(label.clone());\n604\t                param_idx += 1;\n605\t            }\n606\t        }\n607\t        if let Some(min) = priority_min {\n608\t            sql.push_str(&format!(\" AND i.priority >= ?{}\", param_idx));\n609\t            params.push(min.to_string());\n610\t            param_idx += 1;\n611\t        }\n612\t        if let Some(max) = priority_max {\n613\t            sql.push_str(&format!(\" AND i.priority <= ?{}\", param_idx));\n614\t            params.push(max.to_string());\n615\t            param_idx += 1;\n616\t        }\n617\t        sql.push_str(\" ORDER BY i.priority ASC, i.created_at ASC\");\n618\t        if limit > 0 {\n619\t            sql.push_str(&format!(\" LIMIT {}\", limit));\n620\t        }\n621\t        let conn = self.conn.lock().unwrap();\n622\t        let mut stmt = conn.prepare(&sql)?;\n623\t        let param_refs: Vec<&dyn rusqlite::ToSql> = params.iter().map(|p| p as &dyn rusqlite::ToSql).collect();\n624\t        let mut rows = stmt.query(param_refs.as_slice())?;\n625\t        let mut issues = Vec::new();\n626\t        while let Some(row) = rows.next()? {\n627\t            issues.push(Self::row_to_issue_conn(&conn, row)?);\n628\t        }\n629\t        Ok(issues)\n630\t    }\n631\t\n632\t    pub fn get_stats(&self) -> Result<Stats> {\n633\t        let conn = self.conn.lock().unwrap();\n634\t        let total: schema if the issues table doesn't exist yet (avoids DDL lock contention\n        // when NEEDLE workers hold a continuous read lock on the database)\n        let needs_schema: bool = F) -> Result<T>\n    where\n        F: FnOnce(&Transaction) -> Result<T>,\n    {\n        let conn = self.conn.lock().unwrap();\n        let tx = conn.unchecked_transaction()?;\n        match f(&tx) {\n            Ok(result) => {\n                tx.commit()?;\n                drop(conn);\n                Ok(result)\n            }\n            Err(e) => {\n                let _ = tx.rollback();\n                Err(e)\n            }\n        }\n    }\n    pub fn with_immediate_transaction<T, F>(&self, f: F) -> Result<T>\n    where\n        F: Fn(&Connection) -> Result<T>,\n    {\n        let mut attempt = 0;\n        loop {\n            let outcome = {\n                let conn = self.conn.lock().unwrap();\n                match conn.execute_batch(\"BEGIN IMMEDIATE\") {\n                    Err(e) if is_busy_error(&e) && attempt < MAX_RETRIES => None,\n                    Err(e) => return Err(e.into()),\n                    Ok(_) => {\n                        let r = f(&conn);\n                        match &r {\n                            Ok(_) => { let _ = conn.execute_batch(\"COMMIT\"); }\n                            Err(_) => { let _ = conn.execute_batch(\"ROLLBACK\"); }\n                        }\n                        Some(r)\n                    }\n                }\n            };\n            match outcome {\n                Some(r) => return r,\n                None => {\n                    attempt += 1;\n                    std::thread::sleep(Duration::from_millis(RETRY_BASE_MS * attempt as u64));\n                }\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    pub Some(priority) = filter.priority {\n            query.push_str(&format!(\" AND priority = ?{}\", param_idx));\n            params.push(priority.to_string());\n            param_idx += 1;\n        }\n        query.push_str(\" ORDER BY priority ASC, created_at ASC\");\n        if let Some(limit) = filter.limit {\n            query.push_str(&format!(\" LIMIT {}\", limit));\n        }\n        if let Some(offset) = filter.offset {\n            query.push_str(&format!(\" OFFSET {}\", offset));\n        }\n        let conn = self.conn.lock().unwrap();\n        let mut stmt = conn.prepare(&query)?;\n self.with_immediate_transaction(|tx| {\n            let mut updates = Vec::new();\n            let mut params: Vec<String> = Vec::new();\n            if let Some(ref title) = changes.title {\n                updates.push(\"title = ?\");\n                params.push(title.clone());\n            }\n            if let Some(ref description) = changes.description {\n                updates.push(\"description = ?\");\n                params.push(description.clone());\n            }\n            if let Some(ref status) = changes.status {\n                updates.push(\"status = ?\");\n                params.push(status.to_string());\n            }\n            if let Some(priority) = changes.priority {\n                updates.push(\"priority = ?\");\n                params.push(priority.to_string());\n            }\n            if let Some(ref issue_type) = changes.issue_type {\n                updates.push(\"issue_type = ?\");\n                params.push(issue_type.to_string());\n            }\n            if let Some(ref assignee) = changes.assignee {\n                updates.push(\"assignee = ?\");\n                params.push(assignee.clone());\n            }\n            if !updates.is_empty() {\n                updates.push(\"updated_at = ?\");\n                let now = Utc::now().to_rfc3339();\n                params.push(now);\n                let query = format!(\"UPDATE issues SET {} WHERE id = ?\", updates.join(\", \"));\n                let mut all_params = params.clone();\n                all_params.push(id.to_string());\n                let param_refs: Vec<&dyn rusqlite::ToSql> =\n                    all_params.iter().map(|p| p as &dyn rusqlite::ToSql).collect();\n                tx.execute(&query, param_refs.as_slice())?;\n            }\n            Ok(())\n        })\n    }\n\n    pub fn close_issue(&self, id: &str, reason: &str, actor: &str) -> Result<()> {\n        self.with_immediate_transaction(|tx| {\n            let now = Utc::now();\n            tx.execute(\n                \"UPDATE issues SET status = 'closed', closed_at = ?, close_reason = ?, updated_at = ? WHERE id = ?\",\n                params![now.to_rfc3339(), reason, now.to_rfc3339(), id],\n            )?;\n            tx.execute(\n                \"INSERT INTO events (issue_id, event_type, actor, old_value, new_value, created_at) VALUES (?1, 'closed', ?2, NULL, ?3, ?4)\",\n                params![id, actor, reason, now.to_rfc3339()],\n            )?;\n            Ok(())\n        })\n    }\n\n    pub fn count_issues(&self) -> Result<usize> {\n        let conn = self.conn.lock().unwrap();\n        let count: i64 = conn.query_row(\"SELECT COUNT(*) FROM issues\", [], |row| row.get(0))?;\n        Ok(count as usize)\n    }\n\n    pub fn sync_from_jsonl(&self, jsonl_path: &Path) -> Result<ImportResult> {\n        import_jsonl(jsonl_path, |issue| {\n            let existing = self.get_issue(&issue.id)?;\n            match existing {\n                None => {\n                    self.create_issue(issue)?;\n                    Ok(true)\n                }\n                Some(_) => Ok(false),\n            }\n        })\n    }\n\n    pub fn sync_to_jsonl(&self, jsonl_path: &Path, dirty_only: bool) -> Result<usize> {\n        if dirty_only {\n            let result = row.get(7)?;\n        let type_str: String = row.get(9)?;\n        let parse_opt_dt = |idx: usize| -> Result<Option<DateTime<Utc>>> {\n            let s: Option<String> = row.get(idx)?;\n            match s {\n                None => Ok(None),\n                Some(ref val) if val.is_empty() => Ok(None),\n                Some(val) => Ok(Some(parse_datetime(val)?)),\n            }\n        };\n        let id: String = row.get(0)?;\n        Ok(Issue {\n            content_hash: row.get(1)?,\n            title: row.get(2)?,\n            description: row.get(3)?,\n            design: row.get(4)?,\n            acceptance_criteria: row.get(5)?,\n            notes: row.get(6)?,\n            status: Status::from_str(&status_str).unwrap_or(Status::Custom(status_str)),\n            priority: row.get(8)?,\n            issue_type: IssueType::from_str(&type_str).unwrap_or(IssueType::Custom(type_str)),\n            assignee: row.get(10)?,\n            owner: row.get(11)?,\n            estimated_minutes: row.get(12)?,\n            created_at: parse_datetime(row.get(13)?)?,\n            created_by: row.get(14)?,\n            updated_at: parse_datetime(row.get(15)?)?,\n            closed_at: parse_opt_dt(16)?,\n            close_reason: row.get(17)?,\n            closed_by_session: row.get(18)?,\n            due_at: parse_opt_dt(19)?,\n            defer_until: parse_opt_dt(20)?,\n            external_ref: row.get(21)?,\n            source_system: row.get(22)?,\n            source_repo: row.get(23)?,\n            deleted_at: parse_opt_dt(24)?,\n            deleted_by: row.get(25)?,\n            delete_reason: row.get(26)?,\n            original_type: row.get(27)?,\n            compaction_level: row.get(28)?,\n            compacted_at: parse_opt_dt(29)?,\n            compacted_at_commit: row.get(30)?,\n            original_size: row.get(31)?,\n            sender: row.get(32)?,\n            ephemeral: row.get::<_, i32>(33)? != 0,\n            pinned: row.get::<_, i32>(34)? != 0,\n            is_template: row.get::<_, i32>(35)? != 0,\n            labels: Self::load_labels_conn(conn, &id)?,\n            dependencies: Self::load_dependencies_conn(conn, &id)?,\n            comments: Self::load_comments_conn(conn, &id)?,\n            id,\n        })\n    }\n\n    fn load_labels_conn(conn: &Connection, issue_id: &str) &[IssueType], assignee: Option<&str>, labels: &[String], priority_min: Option<i32>, priority_max: Option<i32>, limit: usize) -> Result<Vec<Issue>> {\n        let mut sql = String::from(\n            \"SELECT DISTINCT i.id, i.content_hash, i.title, i.description, i.design, i.acceptance_criteria, i.notes,\n                    i.status, i.priority, i.issue_type, i.assignee, i.owner, i.estimated_minutes,\n                    i.created_at, i.created_by, i.updated_at, i.closed_at, i.close_reason,\n                    i.closed_by_session, i.due_at, i.defer_until, i.external_ref, i.source_system,\n                    i.source_repo, i.deleted_at, i.deleted_by, i.delete_reason, i.original_type,\n                    i.compaction_level, i.compacted_at, i.compacted_at_commit, i.original_size,\n                    i.sender, i.ephemeral, i.pinned, i.is_template\n             FROM issues i\n             LEFT JOIN labels l ON i.id = l.issue_id\n             WHERE i.deleted_at IS NULL\",\n        );\n        let mut params = Vec::new();\n        let mut param_idx = 1;\n        if let Some(q) = query {\n            sql.push_str(&format!(\" AND (i.title LIKE ?{} OR i.description LIKE ?{})\", param_idx, param_idx + 1));\n            params.push(format!(\"%{}%\", q));\n            params.push(format!(\"%{}%\", q));\n            param_idx += 2;\n        }\n        for s in status {\n            sql.push_str(&format!(\" AND i.status = ?{}\", param_idx));\n            params.push(s.to_string());\n            param_idx += 1;\n        }\n        for t in issue_type {\n            sql.push_str(&format!(\" AND i.issue_type = ?{}\", param_idx));\n            params.push(t.to_string());\n            param_idx += 1;\n        }\n        if let Some(a) = assignee {\n            sql.push_str(&format!(\" AND i.assignee = ?{}\", param_idx));\n            params.push(a.to_string());\n            param_idx += 1;\n        }\n        if !labels.is_empty() {\n            let label_conditions: Vec<String> = labels.iter().enumerate().map(|(i, _)| format!(\"l.label = ?{}\", param_idx + i)).collect();\n            sql.push_str(&format!(\" AND ({}) \", label_conditions.join(\" OR \")));\n            for label in labels {\n                params.push(label.clone());\n                param_idx += 1;\n            }\n        }\n        if let Some(min) = priority_min {\n            sql.push_str(&format!(\" AND i.priority >= ?{}\", param_idx));\n            params.push(min.to_string());\n            param_idx += 1;\n        }\n        if let Some(max) = priority_max {\n            sql.push_str(&format!(\" AND i.priority <= ?{}\", param_idx));\n            params.push(max.to_string());\n            param_idx += 1;\n        }\n        sql.push_str(\" ORDER BY i.priority ASC, i.created_at ASC\");\n        if limit > 0 {\n            sql.push_str(&format!(\" LIMIT {}\", limit));\n        }\n        let conn = self.conn.lock().unwrap();\n        let mut stmt = conn.prepare(&sql)?;\n        let param_refs: Vec<&dyn rusqlite::ToSql> = params.iter().map(|p| p as &dyn rusqlite::ToSql).collect();\n        let mut rows = stmt.query(param_refs.as_slice())?;\n        let mut issues = Vec::new();\n        while let Some(row) = rows.next()? {\n            issues.push(Self::row_to_issue_conn(&conn, row)?);\n        }\n        Ok(issues)\n    }\n\n    pub fn get_stats(&self) -> Result<Stats> {\n        let conn = self.conn.lock().unwrap();\n        let total: i64 = conn.query_row(\"SELECT COUNT(*) FROM issues WHERE deleted_at IS NULL\", [], |row| row.get(0))?;\n        let open: i64 = conn.query_row(\"SELECT COUNT(*) FROM issues WHERE status = 'open' AND deleted_at IS NULL\", [], |row| row.get(0))?;\n        let in_progress: i64 = conn.query_row(\"SELECT COUNT(*) FROM issues WHERE status = 'in_progress' AND deleted_at IS NULL\", [], |row| row.get(0))?;\n        let closed: i64 = conn.query_row(\"SELECT COUNT(*) FROM issues WHERE status = 'closed' AND deleted_at IS NULL\", [], |row| row.get(0))?;\n        Ok(Stats { total: total as usize, open: open as usize, in_progress: in_progress as usize, closed: closed as usize })\n    }\n}\n\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, serde::Serialize)]\npub struct Stats {\n    pub total: usize,\n    pub open: usize,\n    pub in_progress: usize,\n    pub closed: usize,\n}\n\nfn is_busy_error(e: &rusqlite::Error) -> bool {\n    matches!(e, rusqlite::Error::SqliteFailure(rusqlite::ffi::Error { code: rusqlite::ErrorCode::DatabaseBusy, .. }, _))\n}\n\nfn parse_datetime(s: String) -> Result<DateTime<Utc>> {\n    Ok(DateTime::parse_from_rfc3339(&s)?.with_timezone(&Utc))\n}\n","numLines":657,"startLine":1,"totalLines":657}}}

The `claim.rs` file already has an implementation of the `claim` function. Looking at it:\n\n1. It takes a `Connection` (not a `Transaction`) - this is passed from `with_immediate_transaction`\n2. It reclaims stale in_progress beads\n3. It selects candidates with scoring\n4. It updates the winner\n5. It inserts an event\n6. It marks dirty\n\nHowever, I see a potential issue. Looking at the task requirements again:\n- \"BEGIN IMMEDIATE, stale reclaim, impact scoring, UPDATE winner\"\n- \"Single transaction, single CLI call\"\n- Acceptance: \"20 concurrent bf claim processes claim distinct beads with no duplicates\"\n\nThe current implementation looks mostly correct, but let me verify the race condition handling. The key issue is that between the SELECT and the UPDATE, another worker could claim the same bead. \n\nLooking at the SQL:\n1. The stale reclaim UPDATE happens first\n2. Then we SELECT candidates\n3. Then we UPDATE the winner\n\nThe problem is that between step 2 and 3, another transaction could have already claimed that bead. We need to ensure that our UPDATE actually claims the bead - we should check that it was actually updated.\n\nLet me look at the current implementation more carefully:\n\n```rust\n// Step 3: Update the winner to in_progress\ntx.execute(\n    \"UPDATE issues\n     SET status = 'in_progress', assignee = ?, updated_at = ?\n     WHERE id = ?\",\n    params![worker, now.to_rfc3339(), &bead_id],\n)?;\n```\n\nThis doesn't check that the bead is still 'open' at the time of update. If another worker claimed it between the SELECT and this UPDATE, we'd be overwriting their claim!\n\nThe fix is to add a condition to the UPDATE WHERE clause:\n```sql\nWHERE id = ? AND status = 'open'\n\n\n```\n\nAnd then check that the UPDATE actually affected a row. If it didn't affect a row (rows_affected == 0), then someone else claimed it and we should return None (or retry).\n\nI see a critical race condition where multiple workers could simultaneously claim the same bead. The current implementation lacks proper synchronization mechanisms to prevent duplicate claims. We need a robust way to ensure that only one worker can successfully claim a bead at a time, likely by implementing a more stringent transaction isolation or a unique constraint mechanism.\n\nThe SQL query's scoring logic is particularly interesting, prioritizing beads with higher downstream impact and more critical float values. This suggests a sophisticated selection process that goes beyond simple first-come, first-served assignment.\n\n```\n\nsql\nORDER BY\n    downstream_impact DESC,\n    critical_float ASC,\n    i.priority ASC,\n    i.created_at ASC\n```\n\nThe multi-level sorting ensures we're selecting the most strategically important beads first. By combining downstream impact, critical float, priority, and creation timestamp, we create a nuanced selection mechanism that prevents workers from grabbing arbitrary open beads.\n\nHowever, the current implementation has a subtle race condition. The SELECT and UPDATE operations are not atomic, which means two workers could potentially target the same bead simultaneously. This could lead to inconsistent claim states and potential data integrity issues.\n\nI need to modify the approach to ensure a truly atomic claim process that prevents concurrent workers from conflicting during bead selection.\n\nThe solution involves adding a conditional check in the UPDATE statement that verifies the bead's status is still 'open' before claiming. By using `WHERE id = ? AND status = 'open'` and validating the rows affected, we can safely handle potential race conditions between selection and assignment.\n\nIf the update fails to modify any rows, it indicates another worker already claimed the bead. In this scenario, we simply return None, signaling no available beads for claiming. The current implementation appears robust and well-structured.","signature":""}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":0,"output_tokens":0},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"66df31d4-ec3e-4dfe-955b-de4eea8c5ecd","uuid":"94beeae8-5fd5-4740-973b-a50062b053b5"}
