Tree direction (down, up, both)\n385\t        #[arg(short, long, default_value = \"down\")]\n386\t        direction: String,\n387\t\n388\t        /// Maximum depth\n389\t        #[arg(long, default_value = \"10\")]\n390\t        max_depth: usize,\n391\t    },\n392\t}\n393\t\n394\t#[derive(Subcommand)]\n395\tpub enum LabelCommands {\n396\t    /// Add label(s) to an issue\n397\t    Add {\n398\t        /// Issue ID\n399\t        id: String,\n400\t\n401\t        /// Label(s) to add (multiple labels supported)\n402\t        #[arg(short, long, required = true, num_args = 1..)]\n403\t        label: Vec<String>,\n404\t    },\n405\t\n406\t    /// Remove label(s) from an issue\n407\t    Remove {\n408\t        /// Issue ID\n409\t        id: String,\n410\t\n411\t        /// Label(s) to remove (multiple labels supported)\n412\t        #[arg(short, long, required = true, num_args = 1..)]\n413\t        label: Vec<String>,\n414\t    },\n415\t\n416\t    /// List labels for an issue or all unique labels\n417\t    List {\n418\t        /// Issue ID (optional - if omitted, lists all unique labels)\n419\t        id: Option<String>,\n420\t    },\n421\t}\n422\t\n423\t#[derive(Subcommand)]\n424\tpub enum CommentsCommands {\n425\t    /// Add a comment\n426\t    Add {\n427\t        /// Issue ID\n428\t        id: String,\n429\t\n430\t        /// Comment text\n431\t        #[arg(required = true, num_args = 1..)]\n432\t        text: Vec<String>,\n433\t    },\n434\t\n435\t    /// List comments for an issue\n436\t    List {\n437\t        /// Issue ID\n438\t        id: String,\n439\t    },\n440\t}\n441\t\n442\t#[derive(Subcommand)]\n443\tpub enum ConfigCommands {\n444\t    /// List all config values\n445\t    List,\n446\t\n447\t    /// Get a specific config value\n448\t    Get {\n449\t        /// Config key\n450\t        key: String,\n451\t    },\n452\t\n453\t    /// Show config file path\n454\t    Path,\n455\t}\n456\t\n457\tpub fn run_cli() -> Result<Cli> {\n458\t    Ok(Cli::try_parse()?)\n459\t}\n460\t\n461\tpub fn run(cli: Cli) -> Result<()> {\n462\t    let workspace = cli.workspace.unwrap_or_else(|| PathBuf::from(\".\"));\n463\t\n464\t    // For init command, we allow the .beads directory to not exist yet\n465\t    match &cli.command {\n466\t        Commands::Init { .. } => {\n467\t            let beads_dir = workspace.join(\".beads\");\n468\t            return match cli.command {\n469\t                Commands::Init { prefix } => cmd_init(&beads_dir, &prefix),\n470\t                _ => unreachable!(),\n471\t            };\n472\t        }\n473\t        _ => {}\n474\t    }\n475\t\n476\t    let beads_dir = find_beads_dir(&workspace)\n477\t        .ok_or_else(|| anyhow!(\"No .beads directory found in {:?}\", workspace))?;\n478\t\n479\t    match cli.command {\n480\t        Commands::Init { prefix } => cmd_init(&beads_dir, &prefix),\n481\t        Commands::Create { title, type_, priority, description, assignee, label } => {\n482\t            cmd_create(&beads_dir, title, type_, priority, description, assignee, label)\n483\t        }\n484\t        Commands::List { status, type_, assignee, priority, limit, format, json } => {\n485\t            let format = if json { \"json\".to_string() } else { format };\n486\t            cmd_list(&beads_dir, status, type_, assignee, priority, limit, &format)\n487\t        }\n488\t        Commands::Show { id, format, json } => {\n489\t            let format = if json { \"json\".to_string() } else { format };\n490\t            cmd_show(&beads_dir, &id, &format)\n491\t        }\n492\t        Commands::Update { id, title, status, priority, assignee } => {\n493\t            cmd_update(&beads_dir, &id, title, status, priority, assignee)\n494\t        }\n495\t        Commands::Close { id, reason } => cmd_close(&beads_dir, &id, &reason),\n496\t        Commands::Reopen { id } => cmd_reopen(&beads_dir, &id),\n497\t        Commands::Delete { id } => cmd_delete(&beads_dir, &id),\n498\t        Commands::Ready { limit, format, json } => {\n499\t            let format = if json { \"json\".to_string() } else { format };\n500\t            cmd_ready(&beads_dir, limit, &format)\n501\t        }\n502\t        Commands::Claim { assignee, model, harness, harness_version, dry_run, format, json } => {\n503\t            let format = if json { \"json\".to_string() } else { format };\n504\t            cmd_claim(&beads_dir, &assignee, model, harness, harness_version, dry_run, &format)\n505\t        }\n506\t        Commands::Sync { flush_only, import_only } => cmd_sync(&beads_dir, flush_only, import_only),\n507\t        Commands::Doctor { repair } => cmd_doctor(&beads_dir, repair),\n508\t        Commands::Count { status } => cmd_count(&beads_dir, status),\n509\t        Commands::Batch { file, json, stdin } => cmd_batch(&beads_dir, file, json, stdin),\n510\t        Commands::Dep(dep) => cmd_dep(&beads_dir, dep),\n511\t        Commands::Label(label) => cmd_label(&beads_dir, label),\n512\t        Commands::Comments(comments) => cmd_comments(&beads_dir, comments),\n513\t        Commands::Search { query, status, type_, assignee, label, priority_min, priority_max, limit, format } => {\n514\t            cmd_search(&beads_dir, query, status, type_, assignee, label, priority_min, priority_max, limit, &format)\n515\t        }\n516\t        Commands::Stats { by_type, by_priority, by_assignee, by_label, format } => {\n517\t            cmd_stats(&beads_dir, by_type, by_priority, by_assignee, by_label, &format)\n518\t        }\n519\t        Commands::Schema { target, format } => cmd_schema(&target, &format),\n520\t        Commands::Config(config) => cmd_config(&beads_dir, config),\n521\t        Commands::Velocity { model, harness, format } => cmd_velocity(&beads_dir, model, harness, &format),\n522\t        Commands::Labels { id, format } => cmd_labels(&beads_dir, &id, &format),\n523\t    }\n524\t}\n525\t\n526\tfn cmd_init(beads_dir: &PathBuf, prefix: &str) -> Result<()> {\n527\t    std::fs::create_dir_all(beads_dir)?;\n528\t\n529\t    let config_path = beads_dir.join(\"config.yaml\");\n530\t    if !config_path.exists() {\n531\t        let config = format!(\n532\t            r#\"issue_prefixes: [{}]\n533\tdefault_priority: 2\n534\tdefault_type: task\n535\tclaim_ttl_minutes: 30\n536\t\"#,\n537\t            prefix\n538\t        );\n539\t        std::fs::write(&config_path, config)?;\n540\t    }\n541\t\n542\t    let metadata_path = beads_dir.join(\"metadata.json\");\n543\t    if !metadata_path.exists() {\n544\t        let metadata = r#\"{\"database\": \"beads.db\", \"jsonl_export\": \"issues.jsonl\"}\"#;\n545\t        std::fs::write(&metadata_path, metadata)?;\n546\t    }\n547\t\n548\t    let db_path = beads_dir.join(\"beads.db\");\n549\t    let _storage = Storage::open(&db_path)?;\n550\t\n551\t    let gitignore_path = beads_dir.join(\".gitignore\");\n552\t    if !gitignore_path.exists() {\n553\t        std::fs::write(&gitignore_path, \"beads.db\\nbeads.db-shm\\nbeads.db-wal\\n\")?;\n554\t    }\n555\t\n556\t    println!(\"Initialized bead-forge workspace in {:?}\", beads_dir);\n557\t    Ok(())\n558\t}\n559\t\n560\tfn cmd_create(\n561\t    beads_dir: &PathBuf,\n562\t    title: String,\n563\t    type_: String,\n564\t    priority: i32,\n565\t    description: Option<String>,\n566\t    assignee: Option<String>,\n567\t    labels: Vec<String>,\n568\t) -> Result<()> {\n569\t    let config = load_config(beads_dir)?;\n570\t    let metadata = load_metadata(beads_dir)?;\n571\t    let db_path = beads_dir.join(&metadata.database);\n572\t    let mut storage = Storage::open(&db_path)?;\n573\t\n574\t    let count = storage.count_issues()?;\n575\t    let prefix = get_default_prefix(&config);\n576\t    let id = crate::id::generate_id(prefix, count);\n577\t\n578\t    let now = Utc::now();\n579\t    let mut issue = Issue::new(id.clone(), title, \".\".to_string());\n580\t    issue.issue_type = IssueType::from_str(type_.as_str()).map_err(|e| anyhow::anyhow!(e))?;\n581\t    issue.priority = Priority(priority);\n582\t    issue.description = description;\n583\t    issue.assignee = assignee;\n584\t    issue.labels = labels;\n585\t\n586\t    storage.create_issue(&issue)?;\n587\t\n588\t    println!(\"{}\", id);\n589\t    Ok(())\n590\t}\n591\t\n592\tfn cmd_list(\n593\t    beads_dir: &PathBuf,\n594\t    status: Option<String>,\n595\t    type_: Option<String>,\n596\t    assignee: Option<String>,\n597\t    priority: Option<i32>,\n598\t    limit: Option<usize>,\n599\t    format: &str,\n600\t) -> Result<()> {\n601\t    let metadata = load_metadata(beads_dir)?;\n602\t    let db_path = beads_dir.join(&metadata.database);\n603\t    let storage = Storage::open(&db_path)?;\n604\t\n605\t    let mut filter = IssueFilter::default();\n606\t    if let Some(s) = status {\n607\t        filter.status = Some(Status::from_str(s.as_str()).map_err(|e| anyhow::anyhow!(e))?);\n608\t    }\n609\t    if let Some(t) = type_ {\n610\t        filter.issue_type = Some(IssueType::from_str(t.as_str()).map_err(|e| anyhow::anyhow!(e))?);\n611\t    }\n612\t    filter.assignee = assignee;\n613\t    filter.priority = priority;\n614\t    // --limit 0 means unlimited\n615\t    filter.limit = limit.and_then(|l| if l == 0 { None } else { Some(l) });\n616\t\n617\t    let issues = storage.list_issues(&filter)?;\n618\t\n619\t    match format {\n620\t        \"json\" => {\n621\t            println!(\"{}\", serde_json::to_string(&issues)?);\n622\t        }\n623\t        _ => {\n624\t            for issue in issues {\n625\t                println!(\"[{}] {} - {} ({})\", issue.id, issue.title, issue.status, issue.priority);\n626\t            }\n627\t        }\n628\t    }\n629\t\n630\t    Ok(())\n631\t}\n632\t\n633\tfn cmd_show(beads_dir: &PathBuf, id: &str, format: &str) -> Result<()> {\n634\t    let metadata = load_metadata(beads_dir)?;\n635\t    let db_path = beads_dir.join(&metadata.database);\n636\t    let storage = Storage::open(&db_path)?;\n637\t\n638\t    let issue = storage.get_issue(id)?.ok_or_else(|| anyhow!(\"Bead not found: {}\", id))?;\n639\t\n640\t    match format {\n641\t        \"json\" => {\n642\t            // Strip dependencies/comments before serializing: NEEDLE's BrDependency\n643\t            // format ({id, title, status, priority, dependency_type}) differs from\n644\t            // bead-forge's Dependency format ({issue_id, depends_on_id, type, ...}).\n645\t            // NEEDLE has #[serde(default)] on the deps field so empty is fine.\n646\t            let mut out = issue;\n647\t            out.dependencies = vec![];\n648\t            out.comments = vec![];\n649\t            // Wrap in array so NEEDLE's parse_single_bead (Vec<Bead> → first) works.\n650\t            println!(\"{}\", serde_json::to_string(&vec![out])?);\n651\t        }\n652\t        _ => {\n653\t            println!(\"ID: {}\", issue.id);\n654\t            println!(\"Title: {}\", issue.title);\n655\t            println!(\"Status: {}\", issue.status);\n656\t            println!(\"Priority: {}\", issue.priority);\n657\t            println!(\"Type: {}\", issue.issue_type);\n658\t            if let Some(desc) = &issue.description {\n659\t                println!(\"Description: {}\", desc);\n660\t            }\n661\t            if let Some(assignee) = &issue.assignee {\n662\t                println!(\"Assignee: {}\", assignee);\n663\t            }\n664\t            if !issue.labels.is_empty() {\n665\t                println!(\"Labels: {}\", issue.labels.join(\", \"));\n666\t            }\n667\t        }\n668\t    }\n669\t\n670\t    Ok(())\n671\t}\n672\t\n673\tfn cmd_update(\n674\t    beads_dir: &PathBuf,\n675\t    id: &str,\n676\t    title: Option<String>,\n677\t    status: Option<String>,\n678\t    priority: Option<i32>,\n679\t    assignee: Option<String>,\n680\t) -> Result<()> {\n681\t    let metadata = load_metadata(beads_dir)?;\n682\t    let db_path = beads_dir.join(&metadata.database);\n683\t    let mut storage = Storage::open(&db_path)?;\n684\t\n685\t    let mut changes = IssueChanges {\n686\t        title,\n687\t        status: status.map(|s| Status::from_str(&s).ok()).flatten(),\n688\t        priority,\n689\t        assignee,\n690\t        ..Default::default()\n691\t    };\n692\t\n693\t    storage.update_issue(id, &changes)?;\n694\t    println!(\"Updated bead {}\", id);\n695\t    Ok(())\n696\t}\n697\t\n698\tfn cmd_close(beads_dir: &PathBuf, id: &str, reason: &str) -> Result<()> {\n699\t    let metadata = load_metadata(beads_dir)?;\n700\t    let db_path = beads_dir.join(&metadata.database);\n701\t    let mut storage = Storage::open(&db_path)?;\n702\t\n703\t    storage.close_issue(id, reason, \"cli\")?;\n704\t    println!(\"Closed bead {}\", id);\n705\t    Ok(())\n706\t}\n707\t\n708\tfn cmd_reopen(beads_dir: &PathBuf, id: &str) -> Result<()> {\n709\t    let metadata = load_metadata(beads_dir)?;\n710\t    let db_path = beads_dir.join(&metadata.database);\n711\t    let mut storage = Storage::open(&db_path)?;\n712\t\n713\t    let changes = IssueChanges {\n714\t        status: Some(Status::Open),\n715\t        ..Default::default()\n716\t    };\n717\t\n718\t    storage.update_issue(id, &changes)?;\n719\t    println!(\"Reopened bead {}\", id);\n720\t    Ok(())\n721\t}\n722\t\n723\tfn cmd_delete(beads_dir: &PathBuf, id: &str) -> Result<()> {\n724\t    let metadata = load_metadata(beads_dir)?;\n725\t    let db_path = beads_dir.join(&metadata.database);\n726\t    let mut storage = Storage::open(&db_path)?;\n727\t\n728\t    storage.with_immediate_transaction(|tx| {\n729\t        tx.execute(\"DELETE FROM issues WHERE id = ?\", [&id])?;\n730\t        Ok(())\n731\t    })?;\n732\t\n733\t    println!(\"Deleted bead {}\", id);\n734\t    Ok(())\n735\t}\n736\t\n737\tfn cmd_ready(beads_dir: &PathBuf, limit: usize, format: &str) -> Result<()> {\n738\t    let metadata = load_metadata(beads_dir)?;\n739\t    let db_path = beads_dir.join(&metadata.database);\n740\t    let storage = Storage::open(&db_path)?;\n741\t\n742\t    let candidates = storage.with_immediate_transaction(|tx| {\n743\t        get_ready_candidates(tx, limit)\n744\t    })?;\n745\t\n746\t    match format {\n747\t        \"json\" => {\n748\t            for candidate in candidates {\n749\t                println!(\"{}\", serde_json::to_string(&candidate)?);\n750\t            }\n751\t        }\n752\t        _ => {\n753\t            for candidate in candidates {\n754\t                println!(\"[{}] {} (priority={}, impact={}, float={})\",\n755\t                    candidate.id, candidate.title, candidate.priority,\n756\t                    candidate.downstream_impact, candidate.critical_float);\n757\t            }\n758\t        }\n759\t    }\n760\t\n761\t    Ok(())\n762\t}\n763\t\n764\tfn cmd_claim(\n765\t    beads_dir: &PathBuf,\n766\t    assignee: &str,\n767\t    _model: Option<String>,\n768\t    _harness: Option<String>,\n769\t    _harness_version: Option<String>,\n770\t    dry_run: bool,\n771\t    format: &str,\n772\t) -> Result<()> {\n773\t    let config = load_config(beads_dir)?;\n774\t    let metadata = load_metadata(beads_dir)?;\n775\t    let db_path = beads_dir.join(&metadata.database);\n776\t    let storage = Storage::open(&db_path)?;\n777\t\n778\t    if dry_run {\n779\t        // Show what would be claimed without actually claiming\n780\t        let candidates = storage.with_immediate_transaction(|tx| {\n781\t            get_ready_candidates(tx, 1)\n782\t        })?;\n783\t\n784\t        if let Some(candidate) = candidates.first() {\n785\t            match format {\n786\t                \"json\" => {\n787\t                    let output = serde_json::json!({\n788\t                        \"bead_id\": candidate.id,\n789\t                        \"title\": candidate.title,\n790\t                        \"priority\": candidate.priority,\n791\t                        \"downstream_impact\": candidate.downstream_impact,\n792\t                        \"critical_float\": candidate.critical_float,\n793\t                        \"assignee\": assignee,\n794\t                        \"dry_run\": true\n795\t                    });\n796\t                    println!(\"{}\", output);\n797\t                }\n798\t                _ => {\n799\t                    println!(\"{} (priority={}, impact={})\", candidate.id, candidate.priority, candidate.downstream_impact);\n800\t                }\n801\t            }\n802\t        } else if format == \"json\" {\n803\t            println!(\"{{}}\");\n804\t        } else {\n805\t            println!(\"No beads available to claim\");\n806\t        }\n807\t    } else {\n808\t        let claim_ttl = config.claim_ttl_minutes;\n809\t        let result = storage.with_immediate_transaction(|tx| {\n810\t            claim(tx, assignee, claim_ttl, Utc::now())\n811\t        })?;\n812\t\n813\t        match result {\n814\t            Some(ClaimResult { bead_id, reclaimed }) => {\n815\t                match format {\n816\t                    \"json\" => {\n817\t                        let output = serde_json::json!({\n818\t                            \"bead_id\": bead_id,\n819\t                            \"reclaimed\": reclaimed,\n820\t                            \"assignee\": assignee\n821\t                        });\n822\t                        println!(\"{}\", output);\n823\t                    }\n824\t                    _ => {\n825\t                        println!(\"{}\", bead_id);\n826\t                    }\n827\t                }\n828\t            }\n829\t            None => {\n830\t                if format == \"json\" {\n831\t                    println!(\"{{}}\");\n832\t                } else {\n833\t                    println!(\"No beads available to claim\");\n834\t                }\n835\t            }\n836\t        }\n837\t    }\n838\t\n839\t    Ok(())\n840\t}\n841\t\n842\tfn cmd_sync(beads_dir: &PathBuf, flush_only: bool, import_only: bool) -> Result<()> {\n843\t    let metadata = load_metadata(beads_dir)?;\n844\t    let db_path = beads_dir.join(&metadata.database);\n845\t    let jsonl_path = beads_dir.join(&metadata.jsonl_export);\n846\t    let mut storage = Storage::open(&db_path)?;\n847\t\n848\t    if import_only {\n849\t        let result = storage.sync_from_jsonl(&jsonl_path)?;\n850\t        println!(\"Imported {} beads\", result.imported);\n851\t    } else if flush_only {\n852\t        let count = storage.sync_to_jsonl(&jsonl_path, false)?;\n853\t        println!(\"Flushed {} beads to JSONL\", count);\n854\t    } else {\n855\t        let count = storage.sync_to_jsonl(&jsonl_path, false)?;\n856\t        println!(\"Synced {} beads to JSONL\", count);\n857\t    }\n858\t\n859\t    Ok(())\n860\t}\n861\t\n862\tfn cmd_doctor(beads_dir: &PathBuf, repair: bool) -> Result<()> {\n863\t    let metadata = load_metadata(beads_dir)?;\n864\t    let db_path = beads_dir.join(&metadata.database);\n865\t\n866\t    if repair {\n867\t        std::fs::remove_file(&db_path)?;\n868\t        let jsonl_path = beads_dir.join(&metadata.jsonl_export);\n869\t        let mut storage = Storage::open(&db_path)?;\n870\t        let result = storage.sync_from_jsonl(&jsonl_path)?;\n871\t        println!(\"Repaired database: imported {} beads from JSONL\", result.imported);\n872\t    } else {\n873\t        let storage = Storage::open(&db_path)?;\n874\t        let count = storage.count_issues()?;\n875\t        println!(\"Database is healthy: {} beads\", count);\n876\t    }\n877\t\n878\t    Ok(())\n879\t}\n880\t\n881\tfn cmd_count(beads_dir: &PathBuf, status: Option<String>) -> Result<()> {\n882\t    let metadata = load_metadata(beads_dir)?;\n883\t    let db_path = beads_dir.join(&metadata.database);\n884\t    let storage = Storage::open(&db_path)?;\n885\t\n886\t    let count = if let Some(s) = status {\n887\t        let filter = IssueFilter {\n888\t            status: Some(Status::from_str(&s).map_err(|e| anyhow::anyhow!(e))?),\n889\t            ..Default::default()\n890\t        };\n891\t        storage.list_issues(&filter)?.len()\n892\t    } else {\n893\t        storage.count_issues()?\n894\t    };\n895\t\n896\t    println!(\"{}\", count);\n897\t    Ok(())\n898\t}\n899\t\n900\tfn cmd_batch(beads_dir: &PathBuf, file: Option<PathBuf>, json: Option<String>, stdin: bool) -> Result<()> {\n901\t    let metadata = load_metadata(beads_dir)?;\n902\t    let db_path = beads_dir.join(&metadata.database);\n903\t    let storage = Storage::open(&db_path)?;\n904\t\n905\t    let ops: Vec<BatchOp> = if let Some(json_str) = json {\n906\t        serde_json::from_str(&json_str)?\n907\t    } else if let Some(file_path) = file {\n908\t        let content = std::fs::read_to_string(&file_path)?;\n909\t        serde_json::from_str(&content)?\n910\t    } else if stdin {\n911\t        parse_stdin()?\n912\t    } else {\n913\t        return Err(anyhow!(\"Must provide --file, --json, or --stdin\"));\n914\t    };\n915\t\n916\t    let results = execute_batch(&storage, ops, beads_dir)?;\n917\t\n918\t    // Print results\n919\t    for result in results {\n920\t        if result.status == \"ok\" {\n921\t            if let Some(id) = result.id {\n922\t                println!(\"[op {}] ok: {}\", result.op, id);\n923\t            } else {\n924\t                println!(\"[op {}] ok\", result.op);\n925\t            }\n926\t        } else {\n927\t            eprintln!(\"[op {}] error: {}\", result.op, result.error.unwrap_or_default());\n928\t        }\n929\t    }\n930\t\n931\t    Ok(())\n932\t}\n933\t\n934\tfn cmd_dep(beads_dir: &PathBuf, dep: DepCommands) -> Result<()> {\n935\t    match dep {\n936\t        DepCommands::Add { issue, depends_on, type_ } => {\n937\t            let metadata = load_metadata(beads_dir)?;\n938\t            let db_path = beads_dir.join(&metadata.database);\n939\t            let storage = Storage::open(&db_path)?;\n940\t            let dep_type = crate::model::DependencyType::from_str(&type_)\n941\t                .map_err(|e| anyhow::anyhow!(e))?;\n942\t            storage.add_dependency(&issue, &depends_on, &dep_type, \"cli\")?;\n943\t            println!(\"Added dependency: {} depends on {} ({})\", issue, depends_on, type_);\n944\t        }\n945\t        DepCommands::Remove { issue, depends_on } => {\n946\t            let metadata = load_metadata(beads_dir)?;\n947\t            let db_path =  Path,\n}\n\npub fn run_cli() -> Result<Cli> {\n    Ok(Cli::try_parse()?)\n}\n\npub fn run(cli: Cli) -> Result<()> {\n    let workspace = cli.workspace.unwrap_or_else(|| PathBuf::from(\".\"));\n\n    // For init command, we allow the .beads directory to not exist yet\n    match &cli.command {\n        Commands::Init { .. } => {\n            let beads_dir = workspace.join(\".beads\");\n            return match cli.command {\n                Commands::Init { prefix } => cmd_init(&beads_dir, &prefix),\n                _ => unreachable!(),\n            };\n        }\n        _ => {}\n    }\n\n    let beads_dir = find_beads_dir(&workspace)\n        .ok_or_else(|| anyhow!(\"No .beads directory found in {:?}\", workspace))?;\n\n    match cli.command {\n        Commands::Init { prefix } => cmd_init(&beads_dir, &prefix),\n        Commands::Create { title, type_, priority, description, assignee, label } => {\n            cmd_create(&beads_dir, title, type_, priority, description, assignee, label)\n        }\n        Commands::List { status, type_, assignee, priority, limit, format, json } => {\n            let format = if json { \"json\".to_string() } else { format };\n            cmd_list(&beads_dir, status, type_, assignee, priority, limit, &format)\n        }\n        Commands::Show { id, format, json } => {\n            let format = if json { \"json\".to_string() } else { format };\n            cmd_show(&beads_dir, &id, &format)\n        }\n        Commands::Update { id, title, status, priority, assignee } => {\n            cmd_update(&beads_dir, &id, title, status, priority, assignee)\n        }\n        Commands::Close { id, reason } => cmd_close(&beads_dir, &id, &reason),\n        Commands::Reopen { id } => cmd_reopen(&beads_dir, &id),\n        Commands::Delete { id } => cmd_delete(&beads_dir, &id),\n        Commands::Ready { limit, format, json } => {\n            let format = if json { \"json\".to_string() } else { format };\n            cmd_ready(&beads_dir, limit, &format)\n        }\n        Commands::Claim { assignee, model, harness, harness_version, dry_run, format, json } => {\n            let format = if json { \"json\".to_string() } else { format };\n            cmd_claim(&beads_dir, &assignee, model, harness, harness_version, dry_run, &format)\n        }\n        Commands::Sync { flush_only, import_only } => cmd_sync(&beads_dir, flush_only, import_only),\n        Commands::Doctor { repair } => cmd_doctor(&beads_dir, repair),\n        Commands::Count { status } => cmd_count(&beads_dir, status),\n        Commands::Batch { file, json, stdin } => cmd_batch(&beads_dir, file, json, stdin),\n        Commands::Dep(dep) => cmd_dep(&beads_dir, dep),\n        Commands::Label(label) => cmd_label(&beads_dir, label),\n        Commands::Comments(comments) => cmd_comments(&beads_dir, comments),\n        Commands::Search { query, status, type_, assignee, label, priority_min, priority_max, limit, format } => {\n            cmd_search(&beads_dir, query, status, type_, assignee, label, priority_min, priority_max, limit, &format)\n        }\n        Commands::Stats { by_type, by_priority, by_assignee, by_label, format } => {\n            cmd_stats(&beads_dir, by_type, by_priority, by_assignee, by_label, &format)\n        }\n        Commands::Schema { target, format } => cmd_schema(&target, &format),\n        Commands::Config(config) => cmd_config(&beads_dir, config),\n        Commands::Velocity { model, harness, format } => cmd_velocity(&beads_dir, model, harness, &format),\n        Commands::Labels { id, format } => cmd_labels(&beads_dir, &id, &format),\n    }\n}\n\nfn cmd_init(beads_dir: &PathBuf, prefix: &str) -> Result<()> {\n    std::fs::create_dir_all(beads_dir)?;\n\n    let config_path = beads_dir.join(\"config.yaml\");\n    if !config_path.exists() {\n        let config = format!(\n            r#\"issue_prefixes: [{}]\ndefault_priority: 2\ndefault_type: task\nclaim_ttl_minutes: 30\n\"#,\n            prefix\n        );\n        std::fs::write(&config_path, config)?;\n    }\n\n    let metadata_path = beads_dir.join(\"metadata.json\");\n    if !metadata_path.exists() {\n        let metadata = r#\"{\"database\": \"beads.db\", \"jsonl_export\": \"issues.jsonl\"}\"#;\n        std::fs::write(&metadata_path, metadata)?;\n    }\n\n    let db_path = beads_dir.join(\"beads.db\");\n    let _storage = Storage::open(&db_path)?;\n\n    let gitignore_path = beads_dir.join(\".gitignore\");\n    if !gitignore_path.exists() {\n        std::fs::write(&gitignore_path, \"beads.db\\nbeads.db-shm\\nbeads.db-wal\\n\")?;\n    }\n\n    println!(\"Initialized bead-forge workspace in {:?}\", beads_dir);\n    Ok(())\n}\n\nfn cmd_create(\n    beads_dir: &PathBuf,\n    title: String,\n    type_: String,\n    priority: i32,\n    description: Option<String>,\n    assignee: Option<String>,\n    labels: Vec<String>,\n) -> Result<()> {\n    let config = load_config(beads_dir)?;\n    let metadata = load_metadata(beads_dir)?;\n    let db_path = beads_dir.join(&metadata.database);\n    let mut storage = Storage::open(&db_path)?;\n\n    let count = storage.count_issues()?;\n    let prefix = get_default_prefix(&config);\n    let id = crate::id::generate_id(prefix, count);\n\n    let now = Utc::now();\n    let mut issue = Issue::new(id.clone(), title, \".\".to_string());\n    issue.issue_type = IssueType::from_str(type_.as_str()).map_err(|e| anyhow::anyhow!(e))?;\n    issue.priority = Priority(priority);\n    issue.description = description;\n    issue.assignee = assignee;\n    issue.labels = labels;\n\n    storage.create_issue(&issue)?;\n\n    println!(\"{}\", id);\n    Ok(())\n}\n\nfn cmd_list(\n    beads_dir: &PathBuf,\n    status: Option<String>,\n    type_: Option<String>,\n    assignee: Option<String>,\n    priority: Option<i32>,\n    limit: Option<usize>,\n    format: &str,\n) -> Result<()> {\n    let metadata = load_metadata(beads_dir)?;\n    let db_path = beads_dir.join(&metadata.database);\n    let storage = Storage::open(&db_path)?;\n\n    let mut filter = IssueFilter::default();\n    if let Some(s) = status {\n        filter.status = Some(Status::from_str(s.as_str()).map_err(|e| anyhow::anyhow!(e))?);\n    }\n    if let Some(t) = type_ {\n        filter.issue_type = Some(IssueType::from_str(t.as_str()).map_err(|e| anyhow::anyhow!(e))?);\n    }\n    filter.assignee = assignee;\n    filter.priority = priority;\n    // --limit 0 means unlimited\n    filter.limit = limit.and_then(|l| if l == 0 { None } else { Some(l) });\n\n    let issues = storage.list_issues(&filter)?;\n\n    match format {\n        \"json\" => {\n            println!(\"{}\", serde_json::to_string(&issues)?);\n        }\n        _ => {\n            for issue in issues {\n                println!(\"[{}] {} - {} ({})\", issue.id, issue.title, issue.status, issue.priority);\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    Ok(())\n}\n\nfn cmd_show(beads_dir: &PathBuf, id: &str, format: &str) -> Result<()> {\n    let metadata = load_metadata(beads_dir)?;\n    let db_path = beads_dir.join(&metadata.database);\n    let storage = Storage::open(&db_path)?;\n\n    let issue = storage.get_issue(id)?.ok_or_else(|| anyhow!(\"Bead not found: {}\", id))?;\n\n    match format {\n        \"json\" => {\n            // Strip dependencies/comments before serializing: NEEDLE's BrDependency\n            // format ({id, title, status, priority, dependency_type}) differs from\n            // bead-forge's Dependency format ({issue_id, depends_on_id, type, ...}).\n            // NEEDLE has #[serde(default)] on the deps field so empty is fine.\n            let mut out = issue;\n            out.dependencies = vec![];\n            out.comments = vec![];\n            // Wrap in array so NEEDLE's parse_single_bead (Vec<Bead> → first) works.\n            println!(\"{}\", serde_json::to_string(&vec![out])?);\n        }\n        _ => {\n            println!(\"ID: {}\", issue.id);\n            println!(\"Title: {}\", issue.title);\n            println!(\"Status: {}\", issue.status);\n            println!(\"Priority: {}\", issue.priority);\n            println!(\"Type: {}\", issue.issue_type);\n            if let Some(desc) = &issue.description {\n                println!(\"Description: {}\", desc);\n            }\n            if let Some(assignee) = &issue.assignee {\n                println!(\"Assignee: {}\", assignee);\n            }\n            if !issue.labels.is_empty() {\n                println!(\"Labels: {}\", issue.labels.join(\", \"));\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    Ok(())\n}\n\nfn cmd_update(\n    beads_dir: &PathBuf,\n    id: &str,\n    title: Option<String>,\n    status: Option<String>,\n    priority: Option<i32>,\n    assignee: Option<String>,\n) -> Result<()> {\n    let metadata = load_metadata(beads_dir)?;\n    let db_path = beads_dir.join(&metadata.database);\n    let mut storage = Storage::open(&db_path)?;\n\n    let mut changes = IssueChanges {\n        title,\n        status: status.map(|s| Status::from_str(&s).ok()).flatten(),\n        priority,\n        assignee,\n        ..Default::default()\n    };\n\n    storage.update_issue(id, &changes)?;\n    println!(\"Updated bead {}\", id);\n    Ok(())\n}\n\nfn cmd_close(beads_dir: &PathBuf, id: &str, reason: &str) -> Result<()> {\n    let metadata = load_metadata(beads_dir)?;\n    let db_path = beads_dir.join(&metadata.database);\n    let mut storage = Storage::open(&db_path)?;\n\n    storage.close_issue(id, reason, \"cli\")?;\n    println!(\"Closed bead {}\", id);\n    Ok(())\n}\n\nfn cmd_reopen(beads_dir: &PathBuf, id: &str) -> {\n        status: Some(Status::Open),\n        ..Default::default()\n    };\n\n    storage.update_issue(id, &changes)?;\n    println!(\"Reopened bead {}\", id);\n    Ok(())\n}\n\nfn cmd_delete(beads_dir: &PathBuf, id: &str) -> Result<()> {\n    let metadata = load_metadata(beads_dir)?;\n    let db_path = beads_dir.join(&metadata.database);\n    let mut storage = Storage::open(&db_path)?;\n\n    storage.with_immediate_transaction(|tx| {\n        tx.execute(\"DELETE FROM issues WHERE id = ?\", [&id])?;\n        Ok(())\n    })?;\n\n    println!(\"Deleted bead {}\", id);\n    Ok(())\n}\n\nfn cmd_ready(beads_dir: &PathBuf, limit: usize, format: &str) -> Result<()> {\n    let metadata = load_metadata(beads_dir)?;\n    let db_path = beads_dir.join(&metadata.database);\n    let storage = Storage::open(&db_path)?;\n\n    let candidates = storage.with_immediate_transaction(|tx| {\n        get_ready_candidates(tx, limit)\n    })?;\n\n    match format {\n        \"json\" => {\n            for candidate in candidates {\n                println!(\"{}\", serde_json::to_string(&candidate)?);\n            }\n        }\n        _               candidate.downstream_impact, candidate.critical_float);\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    Ok(())\n}\n\nfn cmd_claim(\n    beads_dir: &PathBuf,\n    assignee: &str,\n    _model: Option<String>,\n    _harness: Option<String>,\n    _harness_version: Option<String>,\n    dry_run: bool,\n    format: &str,\n) -> Result<()> {\n    let config = load_config(beads_dir)?;\n    let metadata = load_metadata(beads_dir)?;\n    let db_path = beads_dir.join(&metadata.database);\n    let storage = Storage::open(&db_path)?;\n\n    if dry_run {\n        // Show what would be claimed without actually claiming\n        let candidates = storage.with_immediate_transaction(|tx| {\n            get_ready_candidates(tx, 1)\n        })?;\n\n        if let Some(candidate) = candidates.first() {\n            match format {\n                \"json\" => {\n                    let output = serde_json::json!({\n                        \"bead_id\": candidate.id,\n                        \"title\": candidate.title,\n                        \"priority\": candidate.priority,\n                        \"downstream_impact\": candidate.downstream_impact,\n                        \"critical_float\": candidate.critical_float,\n                        \"assignee\": assignee,\n                        \"dry_run\": true\n                    });\n                    println!(\"{}\", output);\n                }\n                _ => {\n                    println!(\"{} (priority={}, impact={})\", candidate.id, candidate.priority, candidate.downstream_impact);\n                }\n            }\n        } else if format == \"json\" {\n            println!(\"{{}}\");\n        } else {\n            println!(\"No beads available to claim\");\n        }\n    } else {\n        let claim_ttl = config.claim_ttl_minutes;\n        let result = storage.with_immediate_transaction(|tx| {\n            claim(tx, assignee, claim_ttl, Utc::now())\n        })?;\n\n        match result {\n          \"bead_id\": bead_id,\n                            \"reclaimed\": reclaimed,\n                            \"assignee\": assignee\n                        });\n                        println!(\"{}\", output);\n                    }\n                    _ => {\n                        println!(\"{}\", bead_id);\n                    }\n                }\n            }\n            None => {\n                if format == \"json\" {\n                    println!(\"{{}}\");\n                } else {\n                    println!(\"No beads available to claim\");\n                }\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    Ok(())\n}\n\nfn cmd_sync(beads_dir: &PathBuf, flush_only: bool, import_only: bool) -> Result<()> {\n    let metadata = load_metadata(beads_dir)?;\n    let = storage.sync_from_jsonl(&jsonl_path)?;\n        println!(\"Imported {} beads\", result.imported);\n    } else if flush_only {\n        let count = storage.sync_to_jsonl(&jsonl_path, false)?;\n        println!(\"Flushed {} beads to JSONL\", count);\n    } else {\n        let count = storage.sync_to_jsonl(&jsonl_path, false)?;\n        println!(\"Synced {} beads to JSONL\", count);\n    }\n\n    Ok(())\n}\n\nfn cmd_doctor(beads_dir: &PathBuf, repair: bool) -> Result<()> {\n    let metadata = load_metadata(beads_dir)?;\n    let db_path = beads_dir.join(&metadata.database);\n\n    if repair {\n storage.sync_from_jsonl(&jsonl_path)?;\n        println!(\"Repaired database: imported {} beads from JSONL\", result.imported);\n    } else {\n        let storage = Storage::open(&db_path)?;\n        let count = storage.count_issues()?;\n        println!(\"Database is healthy: {} beads\", count);\n    }\n\n    Ok(())\n}\n\nfn cmd_count(beads_dir: &PathBuf, status: Option<String>) -> Result<()> {\n    let metadata = load_metadata(beads_dir)?;\n    let db_path = beads_dir.join(&metadata.database);\n    let storage = Storage::open(&db_path)?;\n\n    let count = if let Some(s) = status {\n storage.list_issues(&filter)?.len()\n    } else {\n        storage.count_issues()?\n    };\n\n    println!(\"{}\", count);\n    Ok(())\n}\n\nfn cmd_batch(beads_dir: &PathBuf, file: Option<PathBuf>, json: Option<String>, stdin: bool) -> Result<()> {\n    let metadata = load_metadata(beads_dir)?;\n    let db_path = beads_dir.join(&metadata.database);\n    let storage = Storage::open(&db_path)?;\n\n    let ops: Vec<BatchOp> = if let Some(json_str) = json {\n        serde_json::from_str(&json_str)?\n    } else if let Some(file_path) = file {\n        let content = std::fs::read_to_string(&file_path)?;\n        serde_json::from_str(&content)?\n    } else if stdin {\n        parse_stdin()?\n    } else {\n        return Err(anyhow!(\"Must provide --file, --json, or --stdin\"));\n    };\n\n    let results = execute_batch(&storage, ops, beads_dir)?;\n\n    // Print results\n    for result in results {\n        if result.status == \"ok\" {\n            if let Some(id) = result.id {\n                println!(\"[op {}] ok: {}\", result.op, id);\n            } else {\n                println!(\"[op {}] ok\", result.op);\n            }\n        } else {\n            eprintln!(\"[op {}] error: {}\", result.op, result.error.unwrap_or_default());\n        }\n    }\n\n    Ok(())\n}\n\nfn cmd_dep(beads_dir: &PathBuf, dep: DepCommands) -> Result<()> {\n    match .iter()\n573\t            .filter(|d| matches!(d.dep_type, DependencyType::Blocks))\n574\t            .any(|d| {\n575\t                self.source_repo\n576\t                    .as_ref()\n577\t                    .map(|repo| repo == &d.depends_on_id)\n578\t                    .unwrap_or(false)\n579\t            })\n580\t    }\n581\t\n582\t    pub fn content_hash(&self) -> String {\n583\t format!(\"{:x}\", hash)\n587\t    }\n588\t\n589\t    /// Compare two issues using sync semantics instead of raw struct equality.\n590\t    ///\n591\t    /// This ignores derived or volatile audit fields that would otherwise make\n592\t    /// semantically identical issues look different across import/export\n593\t    /// boundaries, while still comparing the full synced payload other.priority\n605\t            || self.issue_type != other.issue_type\n606\t            || self.assignee != other.assignee\n607\t            || self.owner != other.owner\n608\t            || self.estimated_minutes != other.estimated_minutes\n609\t            || self.created_by != other.created_by\n610\t            || self.closed_at != other.closed_at\n611\t            || self.close_reason other.defer_until\n615\t            || self.external_ref != other.external_ref\n616\t            || self.source_system != other.source_system\n617\t            || self.source_repo != other.source_repo\n618\t            || self.deleted_at != other.deleted_at\n619\t            || self.deleted_by != other.deleted_by\n620\t            || self.delete_reason != other.delete_reason\n621\t            || self.original_type != other.original_type\n622\t            || self.compacted_at != other.compacted_at\n623\t            || self.compacted_at_commit != other.compacted_at_commit\n624\t            || self.original_size != other.original_size\n625\t            || self.sender != other.sender\n626\t            || self.ephemeral != other.ephemeral\n627\t            || self.pinned != other.pinned\n628\t            || self.is_template != other.is_template\n629\t        {\n630\t            return false;\n631\t        }\n632\t\n633\t        // Handle compaction_level serialization quirk where None == 0\n634\t        if self.compaction_level.unwrap_or(0) != other.compaction_level.unwrap_or(0) {\n635\t            return false;\n636\t        }\n637\t\n638\t        // Fast path for relations: if lengths differ, they can't be equal\n639\t        if self.dependencies.len() != other.dependencies.len()\n640\t            || self.comments.len() != other.comments.len()\n641\t        {\n642\t            return false;\n643\t        }\n644\t\n645\t        // Compare labels (order independent)\n646\t        let mut self_labels = self.labels.clone();\n647\t        self_labels.sort_unstable();\n648\t        self_labels.dedup();\n649\t        let mut other_labels = other.labels.clone();\n650\t        other_labels.sort_unstable();\n651\t        other_labels.dedup();\n652\t        if self_labels != other_labels {\n653\t            return false;\n654\t        }\n655\t\n656\t        // Compare dependencies (order independent)\n657\t        let mut self_deps = self.dependencies.clone();\n658\t        self_deps.sort_by(|left, right| {\n659\t            left.issue_id\n660\t                .cmp(&right.issue_id)\n661\t                .then_with(|| left.depends_on_id.cmp(&right.depends_on_id))\n662\t                .then_with(|| left.dep_type.as_str().cmp(right.dep_type.as_str()))\n663\t                .then_with(|| left.created_at.cmp(&right.created_at))\n664\t                .then_with(|| left.created_by.cmp(&right.created_by))\n665\t                .then_with(|| left.metadata.cmp(&right.metadata))\n666\t                .then_with(|| left.thread_id.cmp(&right.thread_id))\n667\t        });\n668\t        let mut other_deps = other.dependencies.clone();\n669\t        other_deps.sort_by(|left, right| {\n670\t            left.issue_id\n671\t                .cmp(&right.issue_id)\n672\t                .then_with(|| left.depends_on_id.cmp(&right.depends_on_id))\n673\t                .then_with(|| left.dep_type.as_str().cmp(right.dep_type.as_str()))\n674\t                .then_with(|| left.created_at.cmp(&right.created_at))\n675\t                .then_with(|| left.created_by.cmp(&right.created_by))\n676\t                .then_with(|| left.metadata.cmp(&right.metadata))\n677\t                .then_with(|| left.thread_id.cmp(&right.thread_id))\n678\t        });\n679\t        if self_deps != other_deps {\n680\t            return false;\n681\t        }\n682\t\n683\t        // Compare comments (order independent)\n684\t        let mut self_comments = self.comments.clone();\n685\t        self_comments.sort_by(|left, right| {\n686\t            left.issue_id\n687\t                .cmp(&right.issue_id)\n688\t                .then_with(|| left.created_at.cmp(&right.created_at))\n689\t                .then_with(|| left.author.cmp(&right.author))\n690\t                .then_with(|| left.body.cmp(&right.body))\n691\t                .then_with(|| left.id.cmp(&right.id))\n692\t        });\n693\t        let mut other_comments = other.comments.clone();\n694\t        other_comments.sort_by(|left, right| {\n695\t            left.issue_id\n696\t                .cmp(&right.issue_id)\n697\t                .then_with(|| left.created_at.cmp(&right.created_at))\n698\t                .then_with(|| left.author.cmp(&right.author))\n699\t                .then_with(|| left.body.cmp(&right.body))\n700\t                .then_with(|| left.id.cmp(&right.id))\n701\t        });\n702\t        if self_comments != other_comments {\n703\t            return false;\n704\t        }\n705\t\n706\t        true\n707\t    }\n708\t\n709\t    /// Check if this issue is a tombstone that has exceeded its TTL.\n710\t    #[must_use]\n711\t    pub fn is_expired_tombstone(&self, retention_days: Option<u64>) -> bool {\n712\t        if self.status != Status::Tombstone {\n713\t            return false;\n714\t        }\n715\t\n716\t        let Some(days) = retention_days else {\n717\t            return false;\n718\t        };\n719\t\n720\t        if days == 0 {\n721\t            return false;\n722\t        }\n723\t\n724\t        let Some(deleted_at) = self.deleted_at else {\n725\t            return false;\n726\t        };\n727\t\n728\t        // Clamp days to a safe maximum to avoid panic in Duration::days().\n729\t        let max_safe_days = 365_u64 * 1000;\n730\t        let days_i64 = i64::try_from(days.min(max_safe_days)).unwrap_or(365_000);\n731\t        let expiration_time = deleted_at + chrono::Duration::days(days_i64);\n732\t        Utc::now() > expiration_time\n733\t    }\n734\t}\n735\t\n736\t/// Epic completion status with child counts.\n737\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize, PartialEq, Eq)]\n738\tpub struct EpicStatus {\n739\t    pub epic: Issue,\n740\t    pub total_children: usize,\n741\t    pub closed_children: usize,\n742\t    pub eligible_for_close: bool,\n743\t}\n744\t\n745\t/// Relationship between two issues.\n746\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize, PartialEq, Eq)]\n747\tpub struct Dependency {\n748\t    /// The issue that has the dependency (source).\n749\t    pub issue_id: String,\n750\t\n751\t    /// The issue being depended on (target).\n752\t    pub depends_on_id: String,\n753\t\n754\t    /// Type of dependency.\n755\t    #[serde(rename = \"type\")]\n756\t    pub dep_type: DependencyType,\n757\t\n758\t    /// Creation timestamp.\n759\t    pub created_at: DateTime<Utc>,\n760\t\n761\t    /// Creator.\n762\t    #[serde(default, skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n763\t    pub created_by: Option<String>,\n764\t\n765\t    /// Optional metadata (JSON).\n766\t    #[serde(default, skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n767\t    pub metadata: Option<String>,\n768\t\n769\t    /// Thread ID for conversation linking.\n770\t    #[serde(default, skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n771\t    pub thread_id: Option<String>,\n772\t}\n773\t\n774\t/// A comment on an issue.\n775\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize, PartialEq, Eq)]\n776\tpub struct Comment {\n777\t    pub id: i64,\n778\t    pub issue_id: String,\n779\t    pub author: String,\n780\t    #[serde(rename = \"text\")]\n781\t    pub body: String,\n782\t    pub created_at: DateTime<Utc>,\n783\t}\n784\t\n785\t/// An event in the issue's history (audit log).\n786\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize, PartialEq, Eq)]\n787\tpub struct Event {\n788\t    pub id: i64,\n789\t    pub issue_id: String,\n790\t    #[serde(rename = \"type\")]\n791\t    pub event_type: EventType,\n792\t    pub actor: String,\n793\t    #[serde(default, skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n794\t    pub old_value: Option<String>,\n795\t    #[serde(default, skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n796\t    pub new_value: Option<String>,\n797\t    #[serde(default, skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n798\t    pub comment: Option<String>,\n799\t    pub created_at: DateTime<Utc>,\n800\t}\n801\t\n802\t/// Changes that can be applied to an issue (non-Serde, for internal updates).\n803\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Default)]\n804\tpub struct IssueChanges {\n805\t    pub title: Option<String>,\n806\t    pub description: Option<String>,\n807\t    pub design: Option<String>,\n808\t    pub acceptance_criteria: Option<String>,\n809\t    pub notes: Option<String>,\n810\t    pub status: Option<Status>,\n811\t    pub priority: Option<i32>,\n812\t    pub issue_type: Option<IssueType>,\n813\t    pub assignee: Option<String>,\n814\t    pub owner: Option<String>,\n815\t    pub estimated_minutes: Option<i32>,\n816\t    pub due_at: Option<DateTime<Utc>>,\n817\t    pub defer_until: Option<DateTime<Utc>>,\n818\t    pub external_ref: Option<String>,\n819\t    pub labels: Option<Vec<String>>,\n820\t}\n821\t\n822\t/// Filter for listing issues (non-Serde, for queries).\n823\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Default)]\n824\tpub struct IssueFilter {\n825\t    pub status: Option<Status>,\n826\t    pub issue_type: Option<IssueType>,\n827\t    pub assignee: Option<String>,\n828\t    pub priority: Option<i32>,\n829\t    pub labels: Option<Vec<String>>,\n830\t    pub limit: Option<usize>,\n831\t    pub offset: Option<usize>,\n832\t}\n833\t\n834\t#[cfg(test)]\n835\tmod tests {\n836\t    use super::*;\n837\t    use chrono::TimeZone;\n838\t\n839\t    #[test]\n840\t Comment, Event from beads_rust. Every field, every skip_serializing_if, every rename must match the JSONL wire format exactly. Acceptance: a br-exported issues.jsonl round-trips through serde_json with zero field loss.\",\n846\t  \"status\": \"in_progress\",\n847\t  \"priority\": 0,\n848\t  \"issue_type\": \"task\",\n849\t \"status_changed\" => Self::StatusChanged,\n            \"priority_changed\" => Self::PriorityChanged,\n            \"assignee_changed\" => Self::AssigneeChanged,\n            \"commented\" => /// Acceptance criteria.\n    #[serde(default, skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n    pub acceptance_criteria: Option<String>,\n\n    /// Additional notes.\n    #[serde(default, skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n    pub notes: Option<String>,\n\n    /// Workflow status.\n    #[serde(default)]\n    pub status: Status,\n\n    /// Priority (0=Critical, 4=Backlog).\n    #[serde(default)]\n    pub priority: Priority,\n\n    /// Issue type (bug, feature, etc.).\n    #[serde(default)]\n    pub issue_type: IssueType,\n\n    /// Assigned user.\n    #[serde(default, skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n    pub assignee: Option<String>,\n\n    /// Issue owner.\n    #[serde(default, skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n    pub owner: Option<String>,\n\n    /// Estimated effort in minutes.\n    #[serde(default, skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n    pub estimated_minutes: Option<i32>,\n\n    /// Creation timestamp.\n    pub created_at: DateTime<Utc>,\n\n    /// Creator username.\n    #[serde(default, skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n    pub created_by: Option<String>,\n\n    /// Last update timestamp.\n    pub updated_at: DateTime<Utc>,\n\n    /// Closure timestamp.\n    #[serde(default, skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n    pub closed_at: Option<DateTime<Utc>>,\n\n    /// Reason for closure.\n    #[serde(default, skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n    pub close_reason: Option<String>,\n\n    /// Session ID that closed this issue.\n    #[serde(default, skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n    pub closed_by_session: Option<String>,\n\n    /// Due date.\n    #[serde(default, skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n    pub due_at: Option<DateTime<Utc>>,\n\n    /// Defer until date.\n    #[serde(default, skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n    pub defer_until: Option<DateTime<Utc>>,\n\n    /// External reference (e.g., JIRA-123).\n    #[serde(default, skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n    pub external_ref: Option<String>,\n\n    /// Source system for imported issues.\n    #[serde(default, skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n    pub source_system: Option<String>,\n\n    /// Source repository for multi-repo support.\n    #[serde(default, skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n    pub source_repo: Option<String>,\n\n    // Tombstone fields\n    #[serde(default, skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n    pub deleted_at: Option<DateTime<Utc>>,\n    #[serde(default, skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n    pub deleted_by: Option<String>,\n    #[serde(default, skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n    pub delete_reason: Option<String>,\n    #[serde(default, skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n    pub original_type: Option<String>,\n\n    // Compaction (legacy/compat)\n    // Note: Always serialize compaction_level as integer (0 when None) for bd conformance\n    // bd's Go sql scanner cannot handle NULL for integer columns\n    #[serde(default, serialize_with = \"serialize_compaction_level\")]\n    pub compaction_level: Option<i32>,\n    #[serde(default, skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n    pub compacted_at: Option<DateTime<Utc>>,\n    #[serde(default, skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n    pub compacted_at_commit: Option<String>,\n    #[serde(default, skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n    pub original_size: Option<i32>,\n\n    // Messaging\n    #[serde(default, skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n    pub sender: Option<String>,\n    #[serde(default, skip_serializing_if = \"is_false\")]\n    pub ephemeral: bool,\n\n    // Context\n    #[serde(default, skip_serializing_if = \"is_false\")]\n    pub pinned: bool,\n    #[serde(default, skip_serializing_if = \"is_false\")]\n    pub is_template: bool,\n\n    // Relations (for export/display, not always in DB table directly)\n    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = \"Vec::is_empty\", default)]\n    pub labels: Vec<String>,\n    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = \"Vec::is_empty\", default)]\n    pub dependencies: Vec<Dependency>,\n    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = \"Vec::is_empty\", default)]\n    pub comments: Vec<Comment>,\n}\n\nimpl Default for Issue {\n    fn default() -> Self {\n        Self {\n            id: String::new(),\n            content_hash: None,\n            title: String::new(),\n            description: None,\n            design: None,\n            acceptance_criteria: None,\n            notes: None,\n            status: Status::default(),\n            priority: Priority::default(),\n            issue_type: IssueType::default(),\n            assignee: None,\n            owner: None,\n            estimated_minutes: None,\n            created_at: Utc::now(),\n            created_by: None,\n            updated_at: Utc::now(),\n            closed_at: None,\n            close_reason: None,\n            closed_by_session: None,\n            due_at: None,\n            defer_until: None,\n            external_ref: None,\n            source_system: None,\n            source_repo: None,\n            deleted_at: None,\n            deleted_by: None,\n            delete_reason: None,\n            original_type: None,\n            compaction_level: None,\n            compacted_at: None,\n            compacted_at_commit: None,\n            original_size: None,\n            sender: None,\n            ephemeral: false,\n            pinned: false,\n            is_template: false,\n            labels: Vec::new(),\n            dependencies: Vec::new(),\n            comments: Vec::new(),\n        }\n    }\n}\n\nimpl Issue {\n    pub fn new(id: String, title: String, source_repo: String) -> Self {\n        let now = Utc::now();\n        Issue {\n            id,\n            title,\n            source_repo: Some(source_repo),\n            created_at: now,\n            updated_at: now,\n            status: Status::default(),\n            priority: Priority::default(),\n            issue_type: IssueType::default(),\n            ..Default::default()\n        }\n    }\n\n    pub fn is_blocked(&self) -> bool {\n        self.dependencies\n            .iter()\n            .filter(|d| matches!(d.dep_type, DependencyType::Blocks))\n            .any(|d| {\n                self.source_repo\n                    .as_ref()\n                    .map(|repo| repo == &d.depends_on_id)\n                    .unwrap_or(false)\n            })\n    }\n\n    pub fn content_hash(&self) -> String {\n        use sha2::{Digest, Sha256};\n        let serialized = serde_json::to_string(self).unwrap_or_default();\n        let hash = Sha256::digest(serialized.as_bytes());\n        format!(\"{:x}\", hash)\n    }\n\n    /// Compare two issues using sync semantics instead of raw struct equality.\n    ///\n    /// This ignores derived or volatile audit fields that would otherwise make\n    /// semantically identical issues look different across import/export\n    /// boundaries, while still comparing the full synced payload including\n    /// labels, dependencies, comments, and user-visible timestamps like `due_at`.\n    #[must_use]\n    pub fn sync_equals(&self, other: &Self) -> bool {\n        if self.id != other.id\n            || self.title != other.title\n            || self.description != other.description\n            || self.design != other.design\n            || self.acceptance_criteria != other.acceptance_criteria\n            || self.notes != other.notes\n            || self.status != other.status\n            || self.priority != other.priority\n            || self.issue_type != other.issue_type\n            || self.assignee != other.assignee\n            || self.owner != other.owner\n            || self.estimated_minutes != other.estimated_minutes\n            || self.created_by != other.created_by\n            || self.closed_at != other.closed_at\n            || self.close_reason != other.close_reason\n            || self.closed_by_session != other.closed_by_session\n            || self.due_at != other.due_at\n            || self.defer_until != other.defer_until\n            || self.external_ref != other.external_ref\n            || self.source_system != other.source_system\n            || self.source_repo != other.source_repo\n            || self.deleted_at != other.deleted_at\n            || self.deleted_by != other.deleted_by\n            || self.delete_reason != other.delete_reason\n            || self.original_type != other.original_type\n            || self.compacted_at != other.compacted_at\n            || self.compacted_at_commit != other.compacted_at_commit\n            || self.original_size != other.original_size\n            || self.sender != other.sender\n            || self.ephemeral != other.ephemeral\n            || self.pinned != other.pinned\n            || self.is_template != other.is_template\n        {\n            return false;\n        }\n\n        // Handle compaction_level serialization quirk where None == 0\n        if self.compaction_level.unwrap_or(0) != other.compaction_level.unwrap_or(0) {\n            return false;\n        }\n\n        // Fast path for relations: if lengths differ, they can't be equal\n        if self.dependencies.len() != other.dependencies.len()\n            || self.comments.len() != other.comments.len()\n        {\n            return false;\n        }\n\n        // Compare labels (order independent)\n        let mut self_labels = self.labels.clone();\n        self_labels.sort_unstable();\n        self_labels.dedup();\n        let mut other_labels = other.labels.clone();\n        other_labels.sort_unstable();\n        other_labels.dedup();\n        if self_labels != other_labels {\n            return false;\n        }\n\n        // Compare dependencies (order independent)\n        let mut self_deps = self.dependencies.clone();\n        self_deps.sort_by(|left, right| {\n            left.issue_id\n                .cmp(&right.issue_id)\n                .then_with(|| left.depends_on_id.cmp(&right.depends_on_id))\n                .then_with(|| left.dep_type.as_str().cmp(right.dep_type.as_str()))\n                .then_with(|| left.created_at.cmp(&right.created_at))\n                .then_with(|| left.created_by.cmp(&right.created_by))\n                .then_with(|| left.metadata.cmp(&right.metadata))\n                .then_with(|| left.thread_id.cmp(&right.thread_id))\n        });\n        let mut other_deps = other.dependencies.clone();\n        other_deps.sort_by(|left, right| {\n            left.issue_id\n                .cmp(&right.issue_id)\n                .then_with(|| left.depends_on_id.cmp(&right.depends_on_id))\n                .then_with(|| left.dep_type.as_str().cmp(right.dep_type.as_str()))\n                .then_with(|| left.created_at.cmp(&right.created_at))\n                .then_with(|| left.created_by.cmp(&right.created_by))\n                .then_with(|| left.metadata.cmp(&right.metadata))\n                .then_with(|| left.thread_id.cmp(&right.thread_id))\n        });\n        if self_deps != other_deps {\n            return false;\n        }\n\n        // Compare comments (order independent)\n        let mut self_comments = self.comments.clone();\n        self_comments.sort_by(|left, right| {\n            left.issue_id\n                .cmp(&right.issue_id)\n                .then_with(|| left.created_at.cmp(&right.created_at))\n                .then_with(|| left.author.cmp(&right.author))\n                .then_with(|| left.body.cmp(&right.body))\n                .then_with(|| left.id.cmp(&right.id))\n        });\n        let mut other_comments = other.comments.clone();\n        other_comments.sort_by(|left, right| {\n            left.issue_id\n                .cmp(&right.issue_id)\n                .then_with(|| left.created_at.cmp(&right.created_at))\n                .then_with(|| left.author.cmp(&right.author))\n                .then_with(|| left.body.cmp(&right.body))\n                .then_with(|| left.id.cmp(&right.id))\n        });\n        if self_comments != other_comments {\n            return false;\n        }\n\n        true\n    }\n\n    /// Check if this issue is a tombstone that has exceeded its TTL.\n    #[must_use]\n    pub fn is_expired_tombstone(&self, retention_days: Option<u64>) -> bool {\n        if self.status != Status::Tombstone {\n            return false;\n        }\n\n        let Some(days) = retention_days else {\n            return false;\n        };\n\n        if days == 0 {\n            return false;\n        }\n\n        let Some(deleted_at) = self.deleted_at else {\n            return false;\n        };\n\n        // Clamp days to a safe maximum to avoid panic in Duration::days().\n        let max_safe_days = 365_u64 * 1000;\n        let days_i64 = i64::try_from(days.min(max_safe_days)).unwrap_or(365_000);\n        let expiration_time = deleted_at + chrono::Duration::days(days_i64);\n        Utc::now() > expiration_time\n    }\n}\n\n/// Epic completion status with child counts.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize, PartialEq, Eq)]\npub struct EpicStatus {\n    pub epic: Issue,\n    pub total_children: usize,\n    pub closed_children: usize,\n    pub eligible_for_close: bool,\n}\n\n/// Relationship between two issues.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize, PartialEq, Eq)]\npub struct Dependency {\n    /// The issue that has the dependency (source).\n    pub issue_id: String,\n\n    /// The issue being depended on (target).\n    pub depends_on_id: String,\n\n    /// Type of dependency.\n    #[serde(rename = \"type\")]\n    pub dep_type: DependencyType,\n\n    /// Creation timestamp.\n    pub created_at: DateTime<Utc>,\n\n    /// Creator.\n    #[serde(default, skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n    pub created_by: Option<String>,\n\n    /// Optional metadata (JSON).\n    #[serde(default, skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n    pub metadata: Option<String>,\n\n    /// Thread ID for conversation linking.\n    #[serde(default, skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n    pub thread_id: Option<String>,\n}\n\n/// A comment on an issue.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize, PartialEq, Eq)]\npub struct Comment {\n    pub id: i64,\n    pub issue_id: String,\n    pub author: String,\n    #[serde(rename = \"text\")]\n    pub body: String,\n    pub created_at: DateTime<Utc>,\n}\n\n/// An event in the issue's history (audit log).\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize, PartialEq, Eq)]\npub struct Event {\n    pub id: i64,\n    pub issue_id: String,\n    #[serde(rename = \"type\")]\n    pub event_type: EventType,\n    pub actor: String,\n    #[serde(default, skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n    pub old_value: Option<String>,\n    #[serde(default, skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n    pub new_value: Option<String>,\n    #[serde(default, skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n    pub comment: Option<String>,\n    pub created_at: DateTime<Utc>,\n}\n\n/// Changes that can be applied to an issue (non-Serde, for internal updates).\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Default)]\npub struct IssueChanges {\n    pub title: Option<String>,\n    pub description: Option<String>,\n    pub design: Option<String>,\n    pub acceptance_criteria: Option<String>,\n    pub notes: Option<String>,\n    pub status: Option<Status>,\n    pub priority: Option<i32>,\n    pub issue_type: Option<IssueType>,\n    pub assignee: Option<String>,\n    pub owner: Option<String>,\n    pub estimated_minutes: Option<i32>,\n    pub due_at: Option<DateTime<Utc>>,\n    pub defer_until: Option<DateTime<Utc>>,\n    pub external_ref: Option<String>,\n    pub labels: Option<Vec<String>>,\n}\n\n/// Filter for listing issues (non-Serde, for queries).\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Default)]\npub struct IssueFilter {\n    pub status: Option<Status>,\n    pub issue_type: Option<IssueType>,\n    pub assignee: Option<String>,\n    pub priority: Option<i32>,\n    pub labels: Option<Vec<String>>,\n    pub limit: Option<usize>,\n    pub offset: Option<usize>,\n}\n\n#[cfg(test)]\nmod tests {\n    use super::*;\n    use chrono::TimeZone;\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_issue_roundtrip_from_br_format() {\n        // This is the exact format br (beads_rust) exports to issues.jsonl\n        let json = r#\"{\n  \"id\": \"bf-1bz\",\n  \"title\": \"Port br-compatible Issue data model with identical Serde attributes (src/model.rs)\",\n  \"description\": \"Port Issue, Status, Priority, IssueType, Dependency, DependencyType, Comment, Event from beads_rust. Every field, every skip_serializing_if, every rename must match the JSONL wire format exactly. Acceptance: a br-exported issues.jsonl round-trips through serde_json with zero field loss.\",\n  \"status\": \"in_progress\",\n  \"priority\": 0,\n  \"issue_type\": \"task\",\n  \"assignee\": \"claude-code-glm-4.7-november\",\n  \"created_at\": \"2026-04-29T23:21:25.206825571Z\",\n  \"created_by\": \"coding\",\n  \"updated_at\": \"2026-04-29T23:51:34.458178699Z\",\n  \"source_repo\": \".\",\n  \"compaction_level\": 0,\n  \"original_size\": 0,\n  \"labels\": [\"deferred\", \"model\", \"phase-1\"]\n}\"#;\n\n        // Deserialize\n        let issue: Issue = serde_json::from_str(json).expect(\"Failed to deserialize br format\");\n\n        // Verify key fields\n        assert_eq!(issue.id, \"bf-1bz\");\n        assert_eq!(issue.status, Status::InProgress);\n        assert_eq!(issue.priority, Priority::CRITICAL);\n        assert_eq!(issue.issue_type, IssueType::Task);\n        assert_eq!(issue.assignee.as_deref(), Some(\"claude-code-glm-4.7-november\"));\n        assert_eq!(issue.source_repo.as_deref(), Some(\".\"));\n        assert_eq!(issue.compaction_level, Some(0));\n        assert_eq!(issue.original_size, Some(0));\n        assert_eq!(issue.labels, vec![\"deferred\", \"model\", \"phase-1\"]);\n\n        // Serialize back and verify\n        let serialized = serde_json::to_string(&issue).expect(\"Failed to serialize\");\n        let roundtrip: Issue = serde_json::from_str(&serialized).expect(\"Failed to deserialize roundtrip\");\n\n        // All fields should match after roundtrip\n        assert_eq!(issue, roundtrip);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_compaction_level_serializes_as_zero_when_none() {\n        // bd conformance: compaction_level must serialize as 0, not null\n        let issue = Issue {\n            id: \"test-1\".to_string(),\n            title: \"Test\".to_string(),\n            created_at: Utc.timestamp_opt(1_700_000_000, 0).unwrap(),\n            updated_at: Utc.timestamp_opt(1_700_000_000, 0).unwrap(),\n            compaction_level: None,\n            ..Default::default()\n        };\n\n        let json = serde_json::to_string(&issue).unwrap();\n        assert!(json.contains(r#\"\"compaction_level\":0\"#));\n        assert!(!json.contains(r#\"\"compaction_level\":null\"#));\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_empty_vectors_skipped_in_serialization() {\n        let issue = Issue {\n            id: \"test-2\".to_string(),\n            title: \"Test\".to_string(),\n            created_at: Utc.timestamp_opt(1_700_000_000, 0).unwrap(),\n            updated_at: Utc.timestamp_opt(1_700_000_000, 0).unwrap(),\n            labels: vec![],\n            dependencies: vec![],\n            comments: vec![],\n            ..Default::default()\n        };\n\n        let json = serde_json::to_string(&issue).unwrap();\n        // Empty vectors should be skipped\n        assert!(!json.contains(r#\"\"labels\":\"#));\n        assert!(!json.contains(r#\"\"dependencies\":\"#));\n        assert!(!json.contains(r#\"\"comments\":\"#));\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_custom_status_roundtrip() {\n        let status: Status =    assert_eq!(event.event_type, EventType::StatusChanged);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_tombstone_status_renamed() {\n        let json =  assert_eq!(dep.dep_type, DependencyType::ParentChild);\n\n        let serialized = serde_json::to_string(&dep.dep_type).unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(serialized, \"\\\"parent-child\\\"\");\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_full_issue_with_all_fields() {\n        let json = r#\"{\n  \"id\": \"bd-full\",\n  \"title\": \"Full Issue\",\n  \"description\": \"Description\",\n  \"design\": \"Design notes\",\n  \"acceptance_criteria\": \"Criteria\",\n  \"notes\": \"Additional notes\",\n  \"status\": \"open\",\n  \"priority\": 1,\n  \"issue_type\": \"bug\",\n  \"assignee\": \"alice\",\n  \"owner\": \"bob\",\n  \"estimated_minutes\": 120,\n  \"created_at\": \"2026-01-01T00:00:00Z\",\n  \"created_by\": \"charlie\",\n  \"updated_at\": \"2026-01-02T00:00:00Z\",\n  \"closed_at\": \"2026-01-03T00:00:00Z\",\n  \"close_reason\": \"Fixed\",\n  \"closed_by_session\": \"session-123\",\n  \"due_at\": \"2026-01-05T00:00:00Z\",\n  \"defer_until\": \"2026-01-06T00:00:00Z\",\n  \"external_ref\": \"JIRA-123\",\n  \"source_system\": \"jira\",\n  \"source_repo\": \"myrepo\",\n  \"deleted_at\": \"2026-01-07T00:00:00Z\",\n  \"deleted_by\": \"admin\",\n  \"delete_reason\": \"Duplicate\",\n  \"original_type\": \"bug\",\n  \"compaction_level\": 1,\n  \"compacted_at\": \"2026-01-08T00:00:00Z\",\n  \"compacted_at_commit\": \"abc123\",\n  \"original_size\": 1000,\n We SELECT a candidate bead\n2. We UPDATE that bead to in_progress\n\nBetween step 1 and 2, another worker could have claimed the same bead. The current UPDATE doesn't check if the bead is still 'open', so we'd overwrite the other worker's claim.\n\nLet me look at the claim function more closely:\n\n```rust\n// Step 3: Update the winner to in_progress\ntx.execute(\n    \"UPDATE issues\n     SET status = 'in_progress', assignee = ?, updated_at = ?\n     WHERE id = ?\",\n    params![worker, now.to_rfc3339(), &bead_id],\n)?;\n```\n\nThis doesn't verify the bead's current status before updating. The fix is to add a condition:\n\n```sql\nWHERE id = ? AND status = 'open'\n```\n\nAnd then check if the UPDATE actually affected a row. Reclaim stale in_progress beads (older than claim_ttl_minutes) back to open\n/// 2. Select candidates with downstream_impact + critical_float scoring\n/// 3. Update the winner to in_progress with assignee=worker\n/// 4. Insert an event\n/// 5. Mark the bead as dirty\n/// 6. Commit\n///\n/// # Arguments\n/// * `tx` - The transaction to use (must be an IMMEDIATE transaction)\n/// * `worker` - The worker ID claiming the bead\n/// * `claim_ttl_minutes` - TTL in minutes after which in_progress beads are reclaimed\n/// * `now` - Current timestamp for staleness calculation\n///\n/// # Returns\n/// * `Ok(Some(claim_result))` - A bead was claimed\n/// * `Ok(None)` - No beads available to claim\n/// * `Err(e)` - Transaction error\npub fn claim(\n    tx: &Connection,\n    worker: &str,\n    claim_ttl_minutes: i64,\n    now: DateTime<Utc>,\n) -> Result<Option<ClaimResult>> {\n    // Step 1: Reclaim stale in_progress beads\n    let stale_cutoff = now - Duration::minutes(claim_ttl_minutes);\n    let reclaimed = tx.execute(\n        \"UPDATE issues\n         SET status = 'open', assignee = NULL, updated_at = ?\n         WHERE status = 'in_progress'\n           AND updated_at < ?\",\n        params![now.to_rfc3339(), stale_cutoff.to_rfc3339()],\n    )?;\n\n    // Step 2: Find candidate beads with impact scoring\n    // Score = downstream_impact + (critical_float / 1000.0)\n    // downstream_impact = count of beads blocked by this one\n    // critical_float = from critical_path_cache (lower is more critical)\n    let mut stmt = tx.prepare(\n        \"SELECT i.id,\n                COALESCE(COUNT(d.issue_id), 0) as downstream_impact,\n                COALESCE(c.float, 999999) as critical_float,\n                i.priority\n         FROM issues i\n         LEFT JOIN dependencies d ON d.depends_on_id = i.id AND d.type IN ('blocks', 'parent-child', 'conditional-blocks', 'waits-for')\n         LEFT JOIN critical_path_cache c ON c.bead_id = i.id\n         WHERE i.status = 'open'\n           AND i.ephemeral = 0\n           AND i.pinned = 0\n           AND i.is_template = 0\n           AND i.deleted_at IS NULL\n         GROUP BY i.id\n         ORDER BY\n             downstream_impact DESC,\n             critical_float ASC,\n             i.priority ASC,\n             i.created_at ASC\n         LIMIT 1\",\n    )?;\n\n    let mut rows = stmt.query([])?;\n\n    if let Some(row) = rows.next()? {\n        let bead_id: String = row.get(0)?;\n\n        // Step 3: Update the winner to in_progress\n        tx.execute(\n            \"UPDATE issues\n             SET status = 'in_progress', assignee = ?, updated_at = ?\n             WHERE id = ?\",\n            params![worker, now.to_rfc3339(), &bead_id],\n        )?;\n\n        // Step 4: Insert event\n        tx.execute(\n            \"INSERT INTO events (issue_id, event_type, actor, new_value, created_at)\n             VALUES (?, 'assignee_changed', '', ?, ?)\",\n            params![&bead_id, worker, now.to_rfc3339()],\n        )?;\n\n        // Step 5: Mark as dirty\n        tx.execute(\n            \"INSERT OR REPLACE INTO dirty_issues (issue_id, marked_at)\n             VALUES (?, ?)\",\n            params![&bead_id, now.to_rfc3339()],\n        )?;\n\n        Ok(Some(ClaimResult {\n            bead_id,\n            reclaimed,\n        }))\n    } else {\n        Ok(None)\n    }\n}\n\n/// Get ready candidates using the same scoring logic as claim().\n///\n/// This returns a list of beads that would be considered for claiming,\n/// ordered by the same scoring formula:\n/// - downstream_impact DESC (more blocking = higher priority)\n/// - critical_float ASC (lower float = more critical)\n/// - priority ASC (0=Critical, 4=Backlog)\n/// - created_at ASC (FIFO tiebreaker)\n///\n/// # Arguments\n/// * `tx` - The transaction to use\n/// * `limit` - Maximum number of candidates to return\n///\n/// # Returns\n/// * `Ok(Vec<ScoredBead>)` - List of scored bead candidates\npub fn get_ready_candidates(tx: &Connection, limit: usize) -> Result<Vec<ScoredBead>> {\n    let mut stmt = tx.prepare(\n        \"SELECT i.id, i.title, i.status, i.priority,\n                COALESCE(COUNT(d.issue_id), 0) as downstream_impact,\n                COALESCE(c.float, 999999) as critical_float,\n                i.created_at\n         FROM issues i\n         LEFT JOIN dependencies d ON d.depends_on_id = i.id AND d.type IN ('blocks', 'parent-child', 'conditional-blocks', 'waits-for')\n         LEFT JOIN critical_path_cache c ON c.bead_id = i.id\n         WHERE i.status = 'open'\n           AND i.ephemeral = 0\n           AND i.pinned = 0\n           AND i.is_template = 0\n           AND i.deleted_at IS NULL\n         GROUP BY i.id\n         ORDER BY\n             downstream_impact DESC,\n             critical_float ASC,\n             i.priority ASC,\n             i.created_at ASC\n         LIMIT ?1\",\n    )?;\n\n    let mut rows = stmt.query(params![limit as i64])?;\n    let mut candidates = Vec::new();\n\n    while let Some(row) = rows.next()? {\n        candidates.push(ScoredBead {\n            id: row.get(0)?,\n            title: row.get(1)?,\n            status: row.get(2)?,\n            priority: row.get(3)?,\n            downstream_impact: row.get(4)?,\n            critical_float: row.get(5)?,\n            created_at: row.get(6)?,\n        });\n    }\n\n    Ok(candidates)\n}\n\n#[cfg(test)]\nmod tests {\n    use super::*;\n    use crate::storage::Storage;\n    use crate::model::{Issue, Status};\n\n    fn setup_test_db() -> (tempfile::NamedTempFile, Storage) {\n        let temp_file = tempfile::NamedTempFile::new().unwrap();\n        let storage = Storage::open(temp_file.path()).unwrap();\n        (temp_file, storage)\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_claim_basic() {\n        let (_temp, mut storage) = setup_test_db();\n\n        // Create an open bead\n        let issue = Issue::new(\"bf-test1\".to_string(), \"Test bead\".to_string(), \".\".to_string());\n        storage.create_issue(&issue).unwrap();\n\n        // Claim it\n        let result = storage.with_immediate_transaction(|tx| {\n            claim(tx, \"worker1\", 30, Utc::now())\n        }).unwrap();\n\n        assert!(result.is_some());\n        let claim_result = result.unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(claim_result.bead_id, \"bf-test1\");\n        assert_eq!(claim_result.reclaimed, 0);\n\n        // Verify the bead is now in_progress\n        let updated = storage.get_issue(\"bf-test1\").unwrap().unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(updated.status, Status::InProgress);\n        assert_eq!(updated.assignee.as_ref().unwrap(), \"worker1\");\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_claim_no_candidates() {\n        let (_temp, mut storage) = setup_test_db();\n\n        // No beads available\n        let result = storage.with_immediate_transaction(|tx| {\n            claim(tx, \"worker1\", 30, Utc::now())\n        }).unwrap();\n\n        assert!(result.is_none());\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_claim_reclaims_stale() {\n        let (_temp, mut storage) = setup_test_db();\n\n        // Create an in_progress bead with old updated_at\n        let mut issue = Issue::new(\"bf-stale\".to_string(), \"Stale bead\".to_string(), \".\".to_string());\n        issue.status = Status::InProgress;\n        issue.assignee = Some(\"worker_old\".to_string());\n        issue.updated_at = Utc::now() - Duration::minutes(60);\n        storage.create_issue(&issue).unwrap();\n\n        // Create an open bead\n        let issue2 = Issue::new(\"bf-open\".to_string(), \"Open bead\".to_string(), \".\".to_string());\n        storage.create_issue(&issue2).unwrap();\n\n        // Claim with 30 min TTL - should reclaim the stale one\n        let result = storage.with_immediate_transaction(|tx| {\n            claim(tx, \"worker_new\", 30, Utc::now())\n        }).unwrap();\n\n        assert!(result.is_some());\n        let claim_result = result.unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(claim_result.reclaimed, 1);\n\n        // The open bead should be claimed (not the stale one, since it was reclaimed to open)\n        // After reclaim, the stale bead is open again, so it could be claimed too\n        // But the open bead has priority by created_at order\n    }\n}\n","structuredPatch":[{"oldStart":89,"oldLines":14,"newStart":89,"newLines":20,"lines":["     if let Some(row) = rows.next()? {","         let bead_id: String = row.get(0)?;"," ","-        //
